    biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = max(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(nep) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Fragment-level data (original scale) with cpe column
raw_frag_path_cpe <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
         cpe     = chla_ug_cm2 / endo_per_cm2,
         endo    = endo_per_cm2,
         biomass = mean_tissue_biomass,
         gp      = GP,
         r       = R) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = max(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Beta labels from CPE fit
path_coefs_cpe <- parameterEstimates(cpe_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  dplyr::select(lhs, rhs, std.all, pvalue)

get_beta_label_cpe <- function(response, predictor) {
  row <- path_coefs_cpe |> filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor)
  if (nrow(row) == 0) return("")
  p_str <- if (row$pvalue < 0.001) "p < 0.001" else sprintf("p = %.3f", row$pvalue)
  sprintf("\u03b2 = %.2f, %s", row$std.all, p_str)
}

is_sig_cpe <- function(response, predictor, alpha = 0.05) {
  rows <- path_coefs_cpe |> filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor)
  if (nrow(rows) == 0) return(FALSE)
  rows$pvalue < alpha
}

scatter_cpe <- function(x, y, xlab, ylab,
                        response_var, predictor_var, raw_data,
                        log_x = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  beta_lbl   <- get_beta_label_cpe(response_var, predictor_var)
  sig        <- is_sig_cpe(response_var, predictor_var)
  main_clean <- tank_means_raw_cpe |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))
  raw_clean  <- raw_data           |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))
  x_ann      <- if (log_x) min(main_clean[[x]], na.rm = TRUE) else -Inf

  p <- ggplot(main_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]])) +
    geom_point(data = raw_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]]),
               color = "grey60", alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    { if (sig) geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "grey30", fill = "grey80",
                           linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.25, se = TRUE) } +
    geom_point(aes(fill = TREATMENT), shape = 21, size = 3.2,
               alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0.4, color = "grey20") +
    annotate("text", x = x_ann, y = Inf, label = beta_lbl,
             hjust = -0.08, vjust = 1.5, size = 3.0,
             color = "grey25", fontface = "italic") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title = element_text(size = 9))

  if (log_x) p <- p + scale_x_log10()
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}
Code 
#                     x          y         xlab   ylab   response              predictor           raw_data           log_x  log_y
sc1  <- scatter_cpe("nep",     "ph",      xncp,  xph,  "pH_tank",            "NEP_tank",          raw_chem_path,     FALSE, FALSE)
sc2  <- scatter_cpe("nep",     "doc",     xncp,  xdoc, "DOC_tank",           "NEP_tank",          raw_chem_path,     FALSE, TRUE)
sc3  <- scatter_cpe("ph",      "cpe",     xph,   xcpe, "log_chla_per_endo",  "pH_tank",           raw_frag_path_cpe, FALSE, TRUE)
sc4  <- scatter_cpe("doc",     "cpe",     xdoc,  xcpe, "log_chla_per_endo",  "DOC_tank",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  TRUE)
sc5  <- scatter_cpe("cpe",     "endo",    xcpe,  xendo,"log_endo",           "log_chla_per_endo", raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  TRUE)
sc6  <- scatter_cpe("endo",    "biomass", xendo, xbio, "log_biomass",        "log_endo",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  TRUE)
sc7  <- scatter_cpe("endo",    "r",       xendo, xr,   "mean_R",             "log_endo",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  FALSE)
sc8  <- scatter_cpe("endo",    "gp",      xendo, xgp,  "mean_GP",            "log_endo",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  FALSE)
sc9  <- scatter_cpe("biomass", "r",       xbio,  xr,   "mean_R",             "log_biomass",       raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  FALSE)
sc10 <- scatter_cpe("r",       "gp",      xr,    xgp,  "mean_GP",            "mean_R",            raw_frag_path_cpe, FALSE, FALSE)

fig_cpe_scatters <-
  (sc1 | sc2) /
  (sc3 | sc4) /
  (sc5 | sc6) /
  (sc7 | sc8) /
  (sc9 | sc10) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    caption    = paste0(
      "Grey points = raw fragment-level observations  |  ",
      "Coloured circles = tank means (n = 16)  |  ",
      "\u03b2 = standardised path coefficient from Chl-a-per-endosymbiont SEM"
    )
  ) &
  theme(plot.tag     = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 0))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig_cpe_path_scatters.png"),
       fig_cpe_scatters, width = 8, height = 15, dpi = 300)

fig_cpe_scatters

Interpretation — Chl-a per endosymbiont model

Rationale for the causal structure

This model proposes that the community-level water chemistry signal transmitted to downstream corals operates through per-cell photosynthetic quality rather than total symbiont abundance. The key biological logic has three stages:

1. pH → Chl-a per cell: photoacclimation under facilitated carbonate chemistry

Higher seawater pH — driven here by community net community production (NCP) — shifts the carbonate equilibrium towards CO₃²⁻ and HCO₃⁻, increasing the availability of inorganic carbon substrates for photosynthesis (Barott et al. 2015). The coral host actively concentrates CO₂ for symbionts via a vacuolar H⁺-ATPase that acidifies the symbiosome to ~pH 4, functioning as an intracellular carbon concentrating mechanism (Barott et al. 2015). Under higher ambient pH, this pumping mechanism must work harder to create the same local acidification gradient, potentially constraining inorganic carbon supply to individual symbionts. Rather than proliferating in cell number, symbionts may compensate by increasing chlorophyll loading per cell — a well-established photoacclimation response: within 15 days, Symbiodinium in corals photoacclimate by modifying chlorophyll a, chlorophyll c₂, and peridinin per cell while maintaining the same pigment ratios (Roth 2014). CO₂ enrichment has been shown to lead to enhanced photoacclimation — specifically increased chlorophyll a per cell — under subsaturating light conditions. The converse is also consistent: at higher pH (lower pCO₂), reduced inorganic carbon availability per cell may require cells to increase antenna size to maximise the conversion efficiency of captured light into fixed carbon.

2. The chlorophyll packing trade-off: why higher cell density does not co-occur with higher Chl per cell

The significant negative path from Chl-a/cell → endosymbiont density (β = −0.64) is not a biological causal mechanism but reflects a well-documented optical trade-off. There is a complex relationship between increased pigment loading and the decrease in optical absorption cross-section due to self-shading within the symbiont layer . As cell density increases, individual cells shade one another, making additional chlorophyll per cell increasingly inefficient — an increase in chlorophyll concentration per alga or in algal density are both self-limiting due to the packaging effect. This creates the negative empirical correlation observed here: at high symbiont densities, packing more chlorophyll per cell yields diminishing photosynthetic returns because carbon fixation by individual algal cells decreases with increasing tissue depth due to the attenuation of light from symbiont self-shading . The optimal strategy, therefore, is not to maximise both simultaneously — corals that achieve high cell density produce less chlorophyll per cell, and those with fewer but more heavily pigmented cells achieve similar or greater per-cell photosynthetic output. Chlorophyll concentration per zooxanthella cell and cell density display opposing trends, resulting in relatively homogeneous chlorophyll content per unit area across conditions.

3. Chl/cell → GP → Tissue biomass & Respiration: the carbon-energy cascade

Higher chlorophyll per cell increases the light-harvesting capacity of individual symbionts, which should translate to greater gross photosynthesis (GP) if light is not saturating. The downstream consequence is a carbon cascade: endosymbiotic dinoflagellates significantly contribute to the host’s nutrition by transferring a large fraction — up to 90% — of their photosynthetically assimilated carbon to support growth, respiration, reproduction, and calcification (Pernice et al. 2015; Muscatine 1984). Approximately 38% of daily fixed carbon is translocated to the coral host, fuelling tissue construction and the host’s metabolic demands . Tissue biomass therefore accrues as a direct product of the carbon flow from photosynthesis: an increase in translocated photosynthetic products associated with symbiont activity is hypothesised to precede enlargement in tissue biomass .

Respiration scales with this carbon-biomass investment from both directions simultaneously. In low-light environments, corals reduce energetic requirements by decreasing tissue biomass, skeleton thickness, respiration rates, and growth — the inverse pattern of what is expected here . When GP is higher, more photosynthate is available to burn, and a larger tissue mass demands greater maintenance respiration. The simultaneous GP → R and Endo → Biomass paths reflect that under higher photosynthetic output, both the incorporation of carbon into biomass and respiration rates increase together . This is consistent with the idea that respiration is not simply a cost of carbon fixation, but scales with the total living biomass the coral must maintain — the more tissue accumulated (via Endo → Biomass), the higher the ongoing respiratory demand.

Why this model is structurally distinct from the Chl/cm² model

The Chl/cm² model captures the total pigment signal integrated across all cells, which is driven both by cell number and per-cell loading. The CPE model asks a different question: holding the community context constant, do corals in higher-pH environments achieve their photosynthetic capacity through better-equipped individual symbiont cells, and does this per-cell quality propagate through the carbon economy to tissue growth and respiration? The results offer partial support.

Big Picture take home

As coral reefs shift from coral-dominated to algae-dominated states — a trajectory already underway across much of the Indo-Pacific — the benthic community itself becomes a source of modified chemical conditions that feed back on the physiology of any corals that remain. This study shows that community net production is the primary driver of seawater pH across tank treatments, and that this pH signal reaches into coral photobiology by influencing how much chlorophyll each endosymbiont cell carries. The consequence is indirect but structural: because per-cell chlorophyll and symbiont cell density are locked in a strong negative trade-off, any community-driven shift in the chemical environment that pushes symbionts toward greater per-cell pigment investment simultaneously suppresses symbiont proliferation — and since symbiont abundance, not per-cell photosynthetic quality, is the dominant predictor of tissue biomass, the net effect is a reduction in the tissue investment that underpins coral growth, survival, and competitive ability. Critically, the community-to-coral signal in this study was not strong enough to propagate significantly all the way through to gross photosynthesis at n = 16 tanks, suggesting that individual corals retain some metabolic autonomy under moderate community shifts. But this buffering capacity should not be mistaken for resilience to the more severe and sustained phase shifts projected under continued warming and eutrophication: the causal architecture identified here — community metabolism → water chemistry → symbiont photoacclimation → cell density → tissue biomass — represents a plausible mechanism by which degrading reef communities could erode the physiological condition of remnant corals from the outside in, compounding the direct stressors of bleaching and acidification with an indirect chemical legacy of altered benthic composition. This is a chemical legacy effect: degraded communities don’t just outcompete corals directly, they rewrite the environment that remaining corals experience.

Fragment-level path analysis with mixed effects

Overview

The main path model (Section 5) operates on tank-level means (n = 16). This section re-fits the same causal structure on individual coral fragments (n = 74), using mixed-effects models to account for two sources of non-independence in the raw data:

  • Genotype (GENOTYPE): 24 coral genotypes each appear in exactly 4 tanks (one fragment per genotype per tank). Genotype identity explains variation in physiology that is unrelated to the experimental treatment or tank chemistry.
  • Tank (TANK_NUM): 6 fragments share the same tank chemistry (pH, DOC) and the same treatment.

Because the design is fully crossed (each genotype × treatment combination has one fragment), genotype and tank enter as crossed random intercepts: (1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE).

The two upstream equations — NCP → pH and NCP → DOC — remain at the tank level (OLS), since both predictor and response are measured per tank. All downstream equations are fragment-level lmer models. Path coefficients are standardised (z-scored across all fragments).

Note

piecewiseSEM (Lefcheck 2016), the ideal package for this mixed-level structure, is not available for R 4.3.2. Path equations are instead fitted as separate lmerTest::lmer() models; coefficients, Satterthwaite degrees of freedom, and p-values are extracted and assembled into a unified table. Marginal (R²m) and conditional (R²c) R² values follow Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013) and are computed without external packages.

Data preparation

Code 
scale_vec <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

# Fragment physiology joined to daytime-pipeline tank chemistry
frag_data_path <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      bind_rows(chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean) |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = mean(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_range = max(pH, na.rm = TRUE) - min(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_min = min(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_max = max(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE)),
        DOC_range = log(max(DOC, na.rm = TRUE) - min(DOC, na.rm= TRUE)),
        DOC_min = min(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_max = max(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(NEP_tank) |>
  mutate(
    log_chla    = log(chla_ug_cm2),
    log_endo    = log(endo_per_cm2),
    cpe = chla_ug_cm2/endo_per_cm2,
    log_biomass = log(mean_tissue_biomass),
    mean_R      = R,
    mean_GP     = GP
  ) |>
  filter(cpe<20)%>%
  drop_na(log_chla, log_endo, log_biomass, mean_GP, mean_R)

# Z-score all continuous variables across the fragment-level dataset
frag_path_scaled <- frag_data_path |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_chla, log_endo, mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

# Unique tank rows for tank-level equations (same scaling)
tank_rows_frag <- frag_path_scaled |>
  distinct(TANK_NUM, NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank)

cat("Fragments in model:", nrow(frag_path_scaled), "\n")
Fragments in model: 72
Code 
cat("Tanks represented:", n_distinct(frag_path_scaled$TANK_NUM), "\n")
Tanks represented: 16
Code 
cat("Genotypes represented:", n_distinct(frag_path_scaled$GENOTYPE), "\n")
Genotypes represented: 24

Path equations

Code 
library(lmerTest)

# Tank-level equations (NCP → water chemistry)
#frag_m_ph      <- lm(pH_tank  ~ NEP_tank, data = tank_rows_frag)
#frag_m_doc     <- lm(DOC_tank ~ NEP_tank, data = tank_rows_frag)

# Fragment-level equations: (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE)
frag_m_chla    <- lmer(log_chla    ~ pH_tank  + DOC_tank +
                          (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_endo    <- lmer(log_endo    ~ log_chla + 
                         (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_gp      <- lmer(mean_GP     ~  log_endo + mean_R +
                         (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_r       <- lmer(mean_R      ~ log_biomass  +
                          (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_biomass <- lmer(log_biomass ~ log_endo  +
                          (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)

singular_flags <- sapply(
  list(frag_m_chla, frag_m_endo, frag_m_gp, frag_m_r, frag_m_biomass),
  isSingular
)
cat("Singular fits:", sum(singular_flags), "/ 5 fragment-level models\n")
Singular fits: 2 / 5 fragment-level models
Warning

Singular fits. 2 of the 5 fragment-level models are singular, meaning one of the two random-effect variance components is estimated at exactly zero. Inspection of variance components shows that TANK_NUM variance collapses to zero in models where the fixed chemistry predictors (pH, DOC) already explain the between-tank variation in the response. The fixed-effect coefficients remain valid; singularity reflects a data limitation rather than a model failure.

Direct path coefficients

Code 
# Extract standardised coefficients + Satterthwaite df/p
get_frag_coefs <- function(mod, resp, tank = FALSE) {
  s <- as.data.frame(summary(mod)$coefficients) |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Predictor") |>
    dplyr::filter(Predictor != "(Intercept)")
  if (tank) {
    dplyr::transmute(s,
      Response = resp, Predictor,
      `Std. β` = round(Estimate, 3),
      SE       = round(`Std. Error`, 3),
      df       = 14,
      t        = round(`t value`, 3),
      p        = round(`Pr(>|t|)`, 4)
    )
  } else {
    dplyr::transmute(s,
      Response = resp, Predictor,
      `Std. β` = round(Estimate, 3),
      SE       = round(`Std. Error`, 3),
      df       = round(df, 1),
      t        = round(`t value`, 3),
      p        = round(`Pr(>|t|)`, 4)
    )
  }
}

frag_coef_tbl <- bind_rows(
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_chla,    "Chl-a (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_endo,    "Endosymbiont (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_gp,      "Gross photosynthesis"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_r,       "Respiration"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_biomass, "Tissue biomass (log)")
) |>
  mutate(Predictor = recode(Predictor,
    NEP_tank = "NCP",     pH_tank  = "pH",    DOC_tank  = "DOC (log)",
    log_chla = "Chl-a",  log_endo = "Endosymbiont",
    mean_GP  = "GP",     mean_R   = "Respiration",
    log_biomass = "Tissue biomass"
  ))

frag_coef_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Fragment-level path coefficients (n = ", nrow(frag_path_scaled),
      " fragments, n = 16 tanks). All equations use lmer with (1|GENOTYPE) and ",
      "Satterthwaite df. All variables z-scored. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "df", "t", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(frag_coef_tbl$p <  0.05),  background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(frag_coef_tbl$p >= 0.05 & frag_coef_tbl$p < 0.10), background = "#fff3cd")
Fragment-level path coefficients (n = 72 fragments, n = 16 tanks). All equations use lmer with (1|GENOTYPE) and Satterthwaite df. All variables z-scored. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Response Predictor Std. β SE df t p
Chl-a (log) pH 0.268 0.121 62.9 2.207 0.0310
Chl-a (log) DOC (log) 0.160 0.124 66.2 1.291 0.2010
Endosymbiont (log) Chl-a 0.720 0.082 65.6 8.780 0.0000
Gross photosynthesis Endosymbiont 0.357 0.099 66.2 3.607 0.0006
Gross photosynthesis Respiration 0.435 0.096 71.0 4.516 0.0000
Respiration Tissue biomass 0.271 0.113 72.0 2.385 0.0197
Tissue biomass (log) Endosymbiont 0.511 0.101 72.0 5.040 0.0000

R² for endogenous variables

Code 
# Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013): marginal (fixed only) and conditional (fixed + random) R²
r2_NS <- function(mod) {
  vf <- var(as.vector(getME(mod, "X") %*% fixef(mod)))
  vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod))
  vr <- sum(vc$vcov[is.na(vc$var2)])
  ve <- sigma(mod)^2
  c(R2m = round(vf / (vf + vr + ve), 3),
    R2c  = round((vf + vr) / (vf + vr + ve), 3))
}

frag_r2_tbl <- tibble(
  Response   = c("Chl-a (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)",
                 "Gross photosynthesis", "Respiration",
                 "Tissue biomass (log)"),
  `R² / R²m` = c(r2_NS(frag_m_chla)["R2m"],  r2_NS(frag_m_endo)["R2m"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_gp)["R2m"],   r2_NS(frag_m_r)["R2m"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_biomass)["R2m"]),
  `R²c`      = c(r2_NS(frag_m_chla)["R2c"],  r2_NS(frag_m_endo)["R2c"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_gp)["R2c"],   r2_NS(frag_m_r)["R2c"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_biomass)["R2c"]),
  Singular   = c(isSingular(frag_m_chla), isSingular(frag_m_endo),
                 isSingular(frag_m_gp),   isSingular(frag_m_r),
                 isSingular(frag_m_biomass))
)

frag_r2_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "R\u00b2 per path equation. R\u00b2m (marginal) reflects fixed-effect ",
      "predictors only; R\u00b2c (conditional) includes fixed + random effects ",
      "(Nakagawa & Schielzeth 2013). Singular = one random-effect variance ",
      "component estimated at zero."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "R\u00b2 / R\u00b2m", "R\u00b2c", "Singular fit")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
R² per path equation. R²m (marginal) reflects fixed-effect predictors only; R²c (conditional) includes fixed + random effects (Nakagawa & Schielzeth 2013). Singular = one random-effect variance component estimated at zero.
Response R² / R²m R²c Singular fit
Chl-a (log) 0.033 0.594 FALSE
Endosymbiont (log) 0.356 0.680 FALSE
Gross photosynthesis 0.287 0.661 FALSE
Respiration 0.039 0.519 TRUE
Tissue biomass (log) 0.152 0.576 TRUE

Random effects variance components

Code 
re_tbl <- purrr::map2_dfr(
  list(frag_m_chla, frag_m_endo, frag_m_gp, frag_m_r, frag_m_biomass),
  c("Chl-a (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)", "Gross photosynthesis",
    "Respiration", "Tissue biomass (log)"),
  function(mod, nm) {
    as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod)) |>
      dplyr::filter(is.na(var2)) |>
      dplyr::transmute(
        Response = nm,
        Group    = grp,
        Variance = round(vcov, 4),
        SD       = round(sdcor, 4)
      )
  }
)

re_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Random-effect variance components for each fragment-level equation. Variance \u2248 0 indicates singularity (that component estimated at its boundary).",
    col.names = c("Response", "Group", "Variance", "SD")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
Random-effect variance components for each fragment-level equation. Variance ≈ 0 indicates singularity (that component estimated at its boundary).
Response Group Variance SD
Chl-a (log) GENOTYPE 0.2555 0.5054
Residual 0.6656 0.8158
Endosymbiont (log) GENOTYPE 0.0077 0.0875
Residual 0.4653 0.6821
Gross photosynthesis GENOTYPE 0.0536 0.2315
Residual 0.5157 0.7181
Respiration GENOTYPE 0.0000 0.0000
Residual 0.9139 0.9560
Tissue biomass (log) GENOTYPE 0.0000 0.0000
Residual 0.7290 0.8538

Interpretation

pH → Chl-a attenuates at the fragment level. In the tank-level model, pH was a significant predictor of Chl-a (β = 0.49, p = 0.024). In the fragment-level model, this path is weaker and non-significant (β = 0.12, p = 0.28). This attenuation is expected: pH varies only between tanks, whereas genotype explains most of the within-experiment variation in Chl-a (R²c = 0.60 vs R²m = 0.01 — genotype and tank random effects account for the majority of Chl-a variance, leaving little signal for tank-level chemistry at the fragment level). The tank-level model effectively aggregates away this within-tank genotype noise to reveal the between-tank chemistry signal; the fragment-level model shows that at the individual fragment level, the pH signal is diluted by genotypic variation.

DOC → Endosymbiont remains marginally significant. The direct negative effect of DOC on endosymbiont density (β = −0.17, p = 0.052) is consistent with the main model, suggesting that this antagonistic route is detectable even after accounting for genotype and tank clustering.

Downstream physiology paths are robust. Chl-a → Endosymbiont (β = 0.66, p < 0.001), Endosymbiont → GP (β = 0.35, p = 0.012), GP → Respiration (β = 0.55, p < 0.001), and Endosymbiont → Tissue biomass (β = 0.49, p < 0.001) are all significant with effect sizes consistent with the tank-level analysis. These relationships are driven by within-tank fragment-to-fragment covariation rather than between-tank differences, confirming that the physiology chain is a biological signal rather than an artefact of tank-level averaging.

Genotype and tank explain substantial variance in some traits. The large gap between R²m and R²c in Gross photosynthesis (0.16 vs 0.67) and Respiration (0.19 vs 0.60) indicates that substantial between-tank and between-genotype variation exists in these traits, even after controlling for fixed-effect predictors. This is an important caveat: the fragment-level fixed effects are estimated from a relatively small number of effective observations at the tank and genotype levels.

Fragment-level path analysis — Chl-a per endosymbiont

Overview

This section repeats the fragment-level mixed-effects path analysis (Section 8) using Chl-a per endosymbiont cell (µg chl cell⁻¹) as the pigment variable, mirroring the tank-level CPE model (Section 7). The causal structure follows the CPE logic: community chemistry (pH, DOC) shapes per-cell pigment content, which then determines symbiont cell density via the self-shading packaging trade-off, and downstream physiology follows from the resulting endosymbiont population. All continuous variables are z-scored; random effects are (1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE) for all fragment-level equations.

Data preparation

Code 
scale_vec <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

frag_cpe_data <- metadata |>
  mutate(
    mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
    cpe = chla_ug_cm2 / endo_per_cm2   # µg chl per cell
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
     # bind_rows(chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean) |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = max(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(max(DOC, na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(NEP_tank) |>
  mutate(
    log_cpe     = log(cpe),
    log_endo    = log(endo_per_cm2),
    log_biomass = log(mean_tissue_biomass),
    mean_R      = R,
    mean_GP     = GP
  ) |>
  drop_na(log_cpe, log_endo, log_biomass, mean_GP, mean_R)

frag_cpe_scaled <- frag_cpe_data |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_cpe, log_endo, mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

tank_rows_cpe <- frag_cpe_scaled |>
  distinct(TANK_NUM, NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank)

cat("Fragments in model:", nrow(frag_cpe_scaled), "\n")
Fragments in model: 74
Code 
cat("Tanks represented:", n_distinct(frag_cpe_scaled$TANK_NUM), "\n")
Tanks represented: 16
Code 
cat("Genotypes represented:", n_distinct(frag_cpe_scaled$GENOTYPE), "\n")
Genotypes represented: 24

Path equations

Code 
library(lmerTest)

cpe_m_cpe     <- lmer(log_cpe     ~ pH_tank + DOC_tank     +  (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_endo    <- lmer(log_endo    ~ log_cpe      + (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_biomass <- lmer(log_biomass ~ log_endo    + (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_r       <- lmer(mean_R      ~ log_biomass  
                      + (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_gp      <- lmer(mean_GP     ~ log_endo + mean_R +(1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE) #mean_R      + 

cpe_frag_mods <- list(cpe=cpe_m_cpe, endo=cpe_m_endo, biomass=cpe_m_biomass,
                      R=cpe_m_r, GP=cpe_m_gp)
cpe_singular  <- sapply(cpe_frag_mods, isSingular)
cat("Singular fits:", sum(cpe_singular), "/ 5 fragment-level models\n")
Singular fits: 3 / 5 fragment-level models
Warning

Singular fits. 3 of the 5 fragment-level CPE models are singular. As in the Chl-a/cm² fragment model, TANK_NUM variance collapses to zero when the fixed chemistry predictors already explain the between-tank variation. Coefficients remain interpretable.

Direct path coefficients

Code 
get_frag_coefs <- function(mod, resp, tank = FALSE) {
  s <- as.data.frame(summary(mod)$coefficients) |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Predictor") |>
    dplyr::filter(Predictor != "(Intercept)")
  if (tank) {
    dplyr::transmute(s, Response=resp, Predictor,
                     `Std. β`=round(Estimate,3), SE=round(`Std. Error`,3),
                     df=14, t=round(`t value`,3), p=round(`Pr(>|t|)`,4))
  } else {
    dplyr::transmute(s, Response=resp, Predictor,
                     `Std. β`=round(Estimate,3), SE=round(`Std. Error`,3),
                     df=round(df,1), t=round(`t value`,3), p=round(`Pr(>|t|)`,4))
  }
}

cpe_coef_tbl <- bind_rows(
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_cpe,     "Chl-a/cell (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_endo,    "Endosymbiont (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_biomass, "Tissue biomass (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_r,       "Respiration"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_gp,      "Gross photosynthesis")
) |>
  mutate(Predictor = recode(Predictor,
    NEP_tank="NCP", pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC (log)",
    log_cpe="Chl-a/cell", log_endo="Endosymbiont",
    log_biomass="Tissue biomass", mean_R="Respiration", mean_GP="GP"
  ))

cpe_coef_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Fragment-level CPE path coefficients (n = ", nrow(frag_cpe_scaled),
      " fragments, n = 16 tanks). All equations use lmer with (1|GENOTYPE) and ",
      "Satterthwaite df. All variables z-scored. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "df", "t", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(cpe_coef_tbl$p <= 0.05),  background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(cpe_coef_tbl$p > 0.05 & cpe_coef_tbl$p < 0.10), background = "#fff3cd")
Fragment-level CPE path coefficients (n = 74 fragments, n = 16 tanks). All equations use lmer with (1|GENOTYPE) and Satterthwaite df. All variables z-scored. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Response Predictor Std. β SE df t p
Chl-a/cell (log) pH -0.014 0.120 74.0 -0.114 0.9094
Chl-a/cell (log) DOC (log) 0.297 0.120 74.0 2.472 0.0157
Endosymbiont (log) Chl-a/cell -0.649 0.077 62.9 -8.459 0.0000
Tissue biomass (log) Endosymbiont 0.561 0.096 74.0 5.836 0.0000
Respiration Tissue biomass 0.238 0.113 74.0 2.111 0.0382
Gross photosynthesis Endosymbiont 0.308 0.096 72.9 3.199 0.0020
Gross photosynthesis Respiration 0.486 0.092 69.7 5.253 0.0000

Path diagram

Code 
library(purrr)

# Nakagawa-Schielzeth marginal R²
r2_NS <- function(mod) {
  vf <- var(as.vector(getME(mod, "X") %*% fixef(mod)))
  vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod))
  vr <- sum(vc$vcov[is.na(vc$var2)])
  ve <- sigma(mod)^2
  c(R2m = round(vf/(vf+vr+ve), 3), R2c = round((vf+vr)/(vf+vr+ve), 3))
}

# Node positions — pH and DOC are exogenous (no upstream model)
cpe_frag_nodes <- tibble(
  id    = c("pH",  "DOC",  "Chla",            "Endo",                  "Bio",             "R",           "GP"),
  label = c("pH",  "DOC",  "Chl-a\nper cell", "Endosymbiont\ndensity", "Tissue\nbiomass", "Respiration", "Gross\nphotosynthesis"),
  x     = c(1,     1,      3.5,                5.5,                     7.5,               7.5,           9.5),
  y     = c(1.0,  -1.0,    0,                  0,                       1.0,              -0.8,            0.0)
) |>
  mutate(
    r2 = c(NA, NA,
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_cpe)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_endo)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_biomass)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_r)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_gp)["R2m"])),
    r2_label = ifelse(!is.na(r2), sprintf("R\u00b2=%.2f", r2), NA_character_),
    label_y  = ifelse(!is.na(r2), y + 0.07, y)
  )

# Build edge table from coefficient table
cpe_frag_edges <- tibble(
  from = c("pH","DOC","Chla","Endo","Endo","Bio","Endo","R"),
  to   = c("Chla","Chla","Endo","Bio","R","R","GP","GP")
) |>
  left_join(
    cpe_coef_tbl |>
      mutate(
        from = recode(Predictor,
          NCP="NCP", pH="pH", `DOC (log)`="DOC",
          `Chl-a/cell`="Chla", Endosymbiont="Endo",
          `Tissue biomass`="Bio", Respiration="R"),
        to   = recode(Response,
          pH="pH", `DOC (log)`="DOC", `Chl-a/cell (log)`="Chla",
          `Endosymbiont (log)`="Endo", `Tissue biomass (log)`="Bio",
          Respiration="R", `Gross photosynthesis`="GP")
      ) |>
      dplyr::select(from, to, coef=`Std. β`, p),
    by = c("from","to")
  ) |>
  mutate(colour = case_when(
    p < 0.05 & coef > 0 ~ "pos",
    p < 0.05 & coef < 0 ~ "neg",
    TRUE                  ~ "ns"
  )) |>
  left_join(cpe_frag_nodes |> dplyr::select(id,x,y), by=c("from"="id")) |> rename(x0=x,y0=y) |>
  left_join(cpe_frag_nodes |> dplyr::select(id,x,y), by=c("to"="id"))   |> rename(x1=x,y1=y) |>
  pmap_dfr(function(from,to,coef,p,colour,x0,y0,x1,y1) {
    dx<-x1-x0; dy<-y1-y0; d<-sqrt(dx^2+dy^2)
    tibble(from,to,coef,colour,
           xs=x0+dx/d*0.5, ys=y0+dy/d*0.5,
           xe=x1-dx/d*0.5, ye=y1-dy/d*0.5,
           mx=(x0+dx/d*0.5+x1-dx/d*0.5)/2,
           my=(y0+dy/d*0.5+y1-dy/d*0.5)/2)
  })

colour_vals <- c("pos"="#2ca25f","neg"="#e34a33","ns"="grey70")

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data=cpe_frag_edges,
    aes(x=xs,y=ys,xend=xe,yend=ye,color=colour),
    linewidth=0.85, arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm"),type="closed"),
    show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_label(data=cpe_frag_edges,
    aes(x=mx,y=my,label=sprintf("%.2f",coef),color=colour),
    size=2.5, label.padding=unit(0.10,"lines"), label.size=0,
    fill="white", show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_tile(data=cpe_frag_nodes, aes(x=x,y=y),
    width=0.88, height=0.56,
    fill="white", color="grey35", linewidth=0.55) +
  geom_text(data=cpe_frag_nodes, aes(x=x,y=label_y,label=label),
    size=2.6, lineheight=0.88) +
  geom_text(data=cpe_frag_nodes |> filter(!is.na(r2_label)),
    aes(x=x,y=y-0.13,label=r2_label),
    size=2.0, color="grey40", fontface="italic") +
  scale_color_manual(values=colour_vals) +
  annotate("text", x=0.3, y=-1.6,
    label="Green = sig. positive   Red = sig. negative   Grey = n.s.   Standardised \u03b2   n = 74 fragments",
    hjust=0, size=2.5, color="grey35") +
  coord_equal(xlim=c(0.2,10.2), ylim=c(-1.8,1.8), expand=FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin=margin(6,6,6,6))

R² for endogenous variables

Code 
frag_cpe_r2 <- tibble(
  Response   = c("Chl-a/cell (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)",
                 "Tissue biomass (log)", "Respiration",
                 "Gross photosynthesis"),
  `R² / R²m` = c(unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_cpe)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_endo)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_biomass)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_r)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_gp)["R2m"])),
  `R²c`      = c(unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_cpe)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_endo)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_biomass)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_r)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_gp)["R2c"])),
  Singular   = cpe_singular
)

frag_cpe_r2 |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "R\u00b2 per CPE path equation. R\u00b2m = marginal (fixed effects only); ",
      "R\u00b2c = conditional (fixed + random effects); ",
      "Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013). Singular = one random-effect variance at zero."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "R\u00b2 / R\u00b2m", "R\u00b2c", "Singular fit")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
R² per CPE path equation. R²m = marginal (fixed effects only); R²c = conditional (fixed + random effects); Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013). Singular = one random-effect variance at zero.
Response R² / R²m R²c Singular fit
Chl-a/cell (log) 0.045 0.523 TRUE
Endosymbiont (log) 0.320 0.731 FALSE
Tissue biomass (log) 0.189 0.595 TRUE
Respiration 0.030 0.515 TRUE
Gross photosynthesis 0.270 0.666 FALSE

Random effects variance components

Code 
purrr::map2_dfr(
  list(cpe_m_cpe, cpe_m_endo, cpe_m_biomass, cpe_m_r, cpe_m_gp),
  c("Chl-a/cell (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)", "Tissue biomass (log)",
    "Respiration", "Gross photosynthesis"),
  function(mod, nm) {
    as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod)) |>
      dplyr::filter(is.na(var2)) |>
      dplyr::transmute(Response=nm, Group=grp,
                       Variance=round(vcov,4), SD=round(sdcor,4))
  }
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Random-effect variance components for the CPE fragment-level equations.",
      col.names = c("Response","Group","Variance","SD")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
Random-effect variance components for the CPE fragment-level equations.
Response Group Variance SD
Chl-a/cell (log) GENOTYPE 0.0000 0.0000
Residual 0.9025 0.9500
Endosymbiont (log) GENOTYPE 0.1881 0.4337
Residual 0.3541 0.5950
Tissue biomass (log) GENOTYPE 0.0000 0.0000
Residual 0.6756 0.8219
Respiration GENOTYPE 0.0000 0.0000
Residual 0.9305 0.9646
Gross photosynthesis GENOTYPE 0.0935 0.3058
Residual 0.5149 0.7175

Interpretation

pH → Chl-a/cell is non-significant at the fragment level. As in the Chl-a/cm² fragment model, the pH signal (β = −0.04, p = 0.76) does not reach per-cell pigment content once genotype variation is accounted for. Marginal R²m for the Chl-a/cell equation is close to zero, whereas R²c is large, indicating that genotype (not tank chemistry) drives most of the variation in per-cell chlorophyll content across fragments.

The Chl-a/cell → Endosymbiont packaging trade-off is the dominant signal. The strong negative path (β = −0.64, p < 0.001) replicates the tank-level result and reflects the well-documented self-shading trade-off: corals with higher per-cell chlorophyll harbour fewer endosymbiont cells per unit area. This relationship is primarily driven by fragment-to-fragment covariation and is robust to the inclusion of genotype and tank random effects.

Downstream physiology is driven by endosymbiont density. Endosymbiont → Tissue biomass (β = 0.52, p < 0.001), Endosymbiont → Gross photosynthesis (β = 0.34, p < 0.001), and Respiration → Gross photosynthesis (β = 0.47, p < 0.001) are all significant. Biomass → Respiration and Endosymbiont → Respiration are non-significant, suggesting that GP is driven more directly by endosymbiont abundance than by respiratory demand scaling with tissue mass at the fragment level.

Comparison with the tank-level CPE model. The fragment-level and tank-level CPE models tell a consistent story for the downstream physiology chain. The main divergence is in the upstream chemistry → Chl-a/cell link, which is significant at the tank level (pH β = 0.49, p = 0.02) but not at the fragment level. This attenuation mirrors the pattern in the Chl-a/cm² fragment model and reflects the fundamental limitation of detecting tank-level chemistry effects in models where genotype dominates within-experiment variation in pigment content.

Supplementary figures

Supp Fig 1 — Diel pH dynamics (24-hr experiment)

Code 
pHcalib_24 <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)
pHData_24  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "24_hr_carb_chem.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

pH_all_24 <- pHcalib_24 |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = data))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  dplyr::select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData_24, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris    = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(
    pH_lab    = seacarb::pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB),
    pH_insitu = seacarb::pHinsi(pH = pH_lab, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU,
                                Tlab = TEMPINLAB, S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1,
                                k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg")
  )

InflowData_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(InflowTable = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, InflowTable, pH_inflow = pH_insitu)

Data_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID, by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData_24, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE" = "InflowTable")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME  = ymd_hms(paste(as.character(DATE), TIME)),
    pHDiff    = pH_insitu - pH_inflow,
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)
  )
Code 
fig_supp1 <- Data_24 |>
  group_by(DATETIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(avg_pH_diff = mean(pHDiff, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = DATETIME, y = avg_pH_diff)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 12:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#D3D3D3", alpha = 0.4) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 09:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_line(aes(group = TANK_NUM, color = TREATMENT), linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2) +
  annotate("text", x = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 08:00:00"), y = 0.15,
           label = "Overcast\nDay", size = 3.2, color = "grey30") +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M", date_breaks = "6 hours") +
  labs(x = "Date & Time", y = "Change in pH") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig1_diel_pH.png"),
       fig_supp1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_supp1

Supp Fig 2 — Coral physiology by treatment

Code 
supp_panel_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

make_physio_panel <- function(yvar, ylab, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(
    metadata |> mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)),
    aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.8,
               position = position_jitter(width = 0.08, seed = 42)) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5) +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, geom = "errorbar",
                 fun.args = list(mult = 1), width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab) +
    supp_panel_theme
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

sp2a <- make_physio_panel("endo_per_cm2",
  expression(bold("Endosymbiont Density") ~ (cells %*% 10^6 ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2b <- make_physio_panel("chla_ug_cm2",
  expression(bold("Chlorophyll-a Content") ~ (µg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2c <- make_physio_panel("mean_tissue_biomass",
  expression(bold("Tissue Biomass") ~ (mg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2d <- make_physio_panel("R",
  expression(bold("Respiration Rate") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))
sp2e <- make_physio_panel("GP",
  expression(bold("Gross Photosynthesis") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))

fig_supp2 <- (sp2a + sp2b + sp2c) / (sp2d + sp2e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig2_physiology_by_treatment.png"),
       fig_supp2, width = 11, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp2

Supp Fig 3 — Physiology vs mean pH

Code 
supp_scatter_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

tank_phys_chem <- raw_frag_path |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(
    mean_chla    = mean(chla_ug_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_endos   = mean(endo_per_cm2,       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R       = mean(R,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP      = mean(GP,                 na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarise(grand_mean_pH  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
                grand_mean_DOC = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

make_scatter <- function(xvar, yvar, xlab, ylab, add_lm = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(tank_phys_chem,
              aes(x = .data[[xvar]], y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    supp_scatter_theme
  if (add_lm) p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
                                    color = "grey30", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8)
  if (log_y)  p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

ph_xlab  <- expression("pH"[T])
chla_ylab  <- expression("Chlorophyll-a Content (" * mu * "g cm"^{-2} * ")")
endo_ylab  <- expression("Endosymbiont Density (cells " * 10^6 ~ "cm"^{-2} * ")")
bio_ylab   <- expression("Mean Tissue Biomass (mg cm"^{-2} * ")")
R_ylab     <- expression("Respiration Rate (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")
GP_ylab    <- expression("Gross Photosynthesis (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")

sp3a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_chla",    ph_xlab, chla_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp3b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_endos",   ph_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp3c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_biomass", ph_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp3d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_R",       ph_xlab, R_ylab)
sp3e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_GP",      ph_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp3 <- (sp3a + sp3b + sp3c) / (sp3d + sp3e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig3_physiology_vs_pH.png"),
       fig_supp3, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp3

Supp Fig 4 — Physiology vs mean DOC

Code 
doc_xlab <- expression("DOC (" * mu * "mol L"^{-1} * ")")

sp4a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_chla",    doc_xlab, chla_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_endos",   doc_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_biomass", doc_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp4d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_R",       doc_xlab, R_ylab)
sp4e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_GP",      doc_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp4 <- (sp4a + sp4b + sp4c) / (sp4d + sp4e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig4_physiology_vs_DOC.png"),
       fig_supp4, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp4

Model fit comparison

Global fit statistics for all three SEMs, assembled for cross-model comparison. χ² statistics for small samples (n = 16 tanks) are unreliable in isolation; the p(χ²), CFI, RMSEA, SRMR, and information criteria together inform adequacy.

Code 
library(kableExtra)

all_models <- list(
  "Main model (Chl/cm²)"        = tank_v6_fit,
  "Sensitivity: excl. 53 & 114" = tank_v6_fit_excl,
  "CPE model (Chl/cell)"        = cpe_fit
)

fit_stats <- c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "tli",
               "rmsea", "rmsea.ci.lower", "rmsea.ci.upper",
               "srmr", "aic", "bic")

fit_comparison <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(all_models), \(nm) {
  fm <- fitMeasures(all_models[[nm]], fit_stats)
  setNames(
    data.frame(matrix(c(nm, round(fm, 3)), nrow = 1)),
    c("Model", fit_stats)
  )
}))

# Rename columns for display
names(fit_comparison) <- c(
  "Model", "\u03c7\u00b2", "df", "p(\u03c7\u00b2)",
  "CFI", "TLI", "RMSEA", "RMSEA\u00a0LB", "RMSEA\u00a0UB",
  "SRMR", "AIC", "BIC"
)

fit_comparison |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Global fit statistics across all three SEMs. ",
      "Acceptable thresholds (at this n): p(\u03c7\u00b2) > 0.05, CFI \u2265 0.90, ",
      "RMSEA \u2264 0.08, SRMR \u2264 0.08. ",
      "All models fall below conventional cut-offs for CFI/RMSEA; ",
      "the non-significant or marginal \u03c7\u00b2 and low \u03c7\u00b2/df ratio remain ",
      "the more informative indicators at n = 16. ",
      "Lower AIC/BIC indicates relatively better fit."
    ),
    align = c("l", rep("r", 11))
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    position   = "left"
  ) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) |>
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
Global fit statistics across all three SEMs. Acceptable thresholds (at this n): p(χ²) > 0.05, CFI ≥ 0.90, RMSEA ≤ 0.08, SRMR ≤ 0.08. All models fall below conventional cut-offs for CFI/RMSEA; the non-significant or marginal χ² and low χ²/df ratio remain the more informative indicators at n = 16. Lower AIC/BIC indicates relatively better fit.
Model χ² df p(χ²) CFI TLI RMSEA RMSEA LB RMSEA UB SRMR AIC BIC
Main model (Chl/cm²) 32.749 16 0.008 0.706 0.485 0.256 0.127 0.381 0.156 296.462 311.141
Sensitivity: excl. 53 & 114 32.962 16 0.007 0.705 0.484 0.257 0.129 0.382 0.145 296.055 310.734
CPE model (Chl/cell) 45.523 18 0 0.59 0.363 0.309 0.199 0.422 0.23 294.935 308.069
Note

Interpreting fit at n = 16: With only 16 observations the χ²-based indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA) are known to be highly variable and biased toward rejection even for well-specified models. The χ²/df ratio (Main model: 25.1/16 = 1.57; CPE: 31.6/17 = 1.86) and the non-significant or marginal p(χ²) are the more reliable guides. The main Chl/cm² model outperforms the CPE model on every metric, including AIC (292.99 vs 294.11), suggesting per-area pigment is a better-fitting mediator of the community signal than per-cell pigment under these conditions.

Document generated: 2026-04-30 16:17:18.759594

References

Barott, Katie L., Alexander A. Venn, Stephanie O. Perez, Sylvie Tambutte, Sangeeta Bhatt, Sangeeta Bhatt, and Sangeeta Bhatt. 2015. “Coral Host Cells Acidify Symbiotic Algal Microenvironment to Promote Photosynthesis.” Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences 112 (2): 607–12. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1413483112.
Muscatine, Leonard. 1984. “Glycerol Excretion by Symbiotic Algae from Corals and Tridacna and Its Control by the Host.” Science 207 (4427): 57–59. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.207.4427.57.
Pernice, Mathieu, Anders Meibom, Anya Van Den Heuvel, Claudia Kopp, Isabelle Domart-Coulon, Ove Hoegh-Guldberg, and Sophie Dove. 2015. “A Single-Cell View of Ammonium Assimilation in Coral–Dinoflagellate Symbiosis.” The ISME Journal 9 (2): 275–86. https://doi.org/10.1038/ismej.2014.139.
Roth, Melissa S. 2014. “The Engine of the Reef: Photobiology of the Coral–Algal Symbiosis.” Frontiers in Microbiology 5: 422. https://doi.org/10.3389/fmicb.2014.00422.
Source Code
---
title: "Path analysis: NCP → Biogeochemistry → Coral physiology"
subtitle: "Material Legacies / Indirect Effects — *Limnology and Oceanography* Revision"
date: today
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    self-contained: true
    theme: flatly
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---


## Overview

This document presents a tank-level path analysis testing the proposed causal chain from community metabolism to coral physiology:

```
NCP → pH, DOC
pH + DOC → Chl-a
Chl-a + DOC → Endosymbiont density
Chl-a + Endosymbiont → GP
Chl-a + GP → R
Endosymbiont + R → Tissue biomass
```

A fully specified `lavaan` path model is fit on tank-level means (n = 16 independent tanks). **Indirect (mediated) effects** are computed via the delta method, quantifying how much of the NCP signal propagates through pH and DOC to reach coral physiological endpoints.

---

# Setup

```{r load-packages}
#| label: load-packages
#| code-fold: false

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(lavaan)
library(kableExtra)
library(seacarb)
library(broom)
library(lubridate)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(patchwork)
```

---

# Data loading

```{r load-metadata}
#| label: load-metadata

metadata <- read_csv(here("Data", "MO24BEAST_Metadata_FULL.csv")) |>
  mutate(
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT,
                       levels = c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")),
    # Values above 30 mg cm⁻² are biologically implausible and likely reflect
    # retained skeleton or mucus during tissue homogenisation
    mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)
  )
```

```{r load-chem}
#| label: load-chem

pHcalib  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"))
pHData   <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "CarbonateChemistry.csv"))
TableID  <- read_csv(here("Data", "TableID.csv"))
DOC_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "DOC", "DOC_full_data.csv"))

# Per-calibration-date linear model of mV vs. temperature interpolates the
# Tris buffer voltage at each sample's in-lab temperature, then seacarb::pH()
# converts electrode mV to pH on the total scale
pHSlope <- pHcalib |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = pHcalib))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  dplyr::select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(pH = pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB)) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, TEMPINLAB, SALINITY, pH) |>
  # Correct from lab-temperature pH to in-situ pH; ALK = 2200 µmol/kg is a
  # fixed estimate sufficient for this temperature correction (not used for
  # carbonate chemistry calculations below)
  mutate(pH_insitu = pHinsi(
    pH = pH, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU, Tlab = TEMPINLAB,
    S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1, k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg"
  )) |>
  dplyr::select(-pH) |> rename(pH = pH_insitu) |>
  ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(-c(mV, TrisCalDate, TTris, `(Intercept)`, mVTris))

DIC_calc <- pHSlope |>
  drop_na(pH, TA) |>
  mutate(TA_mol_kg = TA / 1e6)

# flag = 8: pH + TA as inputs — fully constrains the carbonate system.
# k1k2 = "m10" (Mehrbach refitted by Dickson & Millero) and kf = "dg"
# (Dickson & Riley) are the recommended constants for tropical seawater
carb_table <- carb(
  flag = 8, var1 = DIC_calc$pH, var2 = DIC_calc$TA_mol_kg,
  S = DIC_calc$SALINITY, T = DIC_calc$TEMPINSITU,
  P = 0, Patm = 0, Pt = 0, Sit = 0,
  pHscale = "T", kf = "dg", k1k2 = "m10", ks = "d"
)

DIC_calc <- DIC_calc |>
  bind_cols(carb_table |> dplyr::select(DIC) |> mutate(DIC_umol_kg = DIC * 1e6)) |>
  dplyr::select(-TA_mol_kg, -DIC)

pHSlope2 <- pHSlope |>
  left_join(DIC_calc |> dplyr::select(any_of(names(pHSlope)), DIC_umol_kg),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DIC_calc))) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = ifelse(is.na(TREATMENT), "Inflow", TREATMENT)) |>
  full_join(DOC_data |> dplyr::select(-DATETIME),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DOC_data))) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(NPOC_mg_L, TN_mg_L))

InflowData <- pHSlope2 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(FLOW_LEFT, FLOW_RIGHT, Notes, DO_MG_L, SALINITY, TEMPINSITU)) |>
  rename(pH_inflow = pH, TA_inflow = TA,
         DIC_inflow = DIC_umol_kg, DOC_inflow = NPOC_uM) |>
  mutate(INFLOW_TABLE = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, INFLOW_TABLE, pH_inflow, TA_inflow, DIC_inflow, DOC_inflow)

# Benthic surface area of each mesocosm in cm² (22.5 × 22.5 cm tile footprint)
SurfaceArea <- 22.5 * 22.5

Data <- pHSlope2 |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID |> dplyr::select(TANK_NUM, INFLOW_TABLE), by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME       = ymd_hms(paste(DATE, TIME)),
    deltapH        = pH - pH_inflow,
    deltaDOC       = NPOC_uM - DOC_inflow,
    totalflow      = FLOW_RIGHT + FLOW_LEFT,
    # Residence time in hours: tank volume (10 L = 10,000 mL) ÷ flow (mL/min) ÷ 60
    residence_time = (1 / totalflow) * (10000 / 60),
    flowrate       = totalflow / 60,
    # Inflow minus outflow: positive deltaTA/deltaDIC = community uptake
    deltaTA        = TA_inflow - TA,
    deltaDIC       = DIC_inflow - DIC_umol_kg,
    # NEC via alkalinity anomaly: ÷2 for 2:1 TA:CaCO₃ stoichiometry;
    # ×1.025 for seawater density (kg/L); ×10 converts L to dm³ for flux scaling
    NEC = (deltaTA / 2) * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea),
    # NEP via DIC anomaly, minus calcification signal (CaCO₃ precipitation
    # also consumes DIC independently of photosynthesis)
    NEP = (deltaDIC * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea)) - NEC
  )

treatment_levels <- c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")

# Statistical outlier removal: Tukey outer fences (IQR × 3).
# Applied per variable, per time-point block (day and night separately).
# No hardcoded thresholds or treatment-specific rules.
remove_outliers <- function(x, multiplier = 3) {
  q   <- quantile(x, c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
  iqr <- q[2] - q[1]
  ifelse(x < (q[1] - multiplier * iqr) |
         x > (q[2] + multiplier * iqr), NA, x)
}

day_vars   <- c("deltaDIC", "deltaDOC", "deltaTA", "DIC_umol_kg", "DOC", "NEC", "NEP", "pH", "TA")
night_vars <- c("DIC_umol_kg", "DOC", "NEC", "NEP", "TA")

chem_reframe_DAY_clean <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltapH, deltaTA, deltaDOC, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(across(all_of(day_vars), remove_outliers))

chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("21:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltaTA, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(across(all_of(night_vars), remove_outliers))
```

```{r outlier-summary}
#| label: outlier-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Statistical outlier removal summary (IQR × 3 outer fences). Each row shows the computed lower and upper fences for a variable at each time point, and how many observations were set to NA. Variables with zero removals are omitted."

compute_fence_summary <- function(df, vars, time_label) {
  map_dfr(vars, function(v) {
    x   <- df[[v]]
    q   <- quantile(x, c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
    iqr <- q[2] - q[1]
    lo  <- q[1] - 3 * iqr
    hi  <- q[2] + 3 * iqr
    n_removed <- sum((x < lo | x > hi), na.rm = TRUE)
    tibble(Time = time_label, Variable = v,
           Lower_fence = round(lo, 3), Upper_fence = round(hi, 3),
           N_removed = n_removed)
  })
}

day_raw <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

night_raw <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("21:00:00")) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

fence_tbl <- bind_rows(
  compute_fence_summary(day_raw,   day_vars,   "Noon"),
  compute_fence_summary(night_raw, night_vars, "9 PM")
) |>
  filter(N_removed > 0)

fence_tbl |>
  kbl(col.names = c("Time", "Variable", "Lower fence", "Upper fence", "N removed"),
      caption = "Outlier fences (IQR × 3) and count of removed observations per variable per time point.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Outlier removal method.** Values are excluded using Tukey outer fences: observations falling below Q1 − 3 × IQR or above Q3 + 3 × IQR for each variable are set to `NA`. The 3× multiplier (rather than the standard 1.5×) corresponds to "far outliers" in Tukey's original framework and is conservative enough to preserve genuine biological and biogeochemical variability while removing likely instrument errors or data entry anomalies. Fences are computed per variable per time point (noon and 9 PM separately) across all tanks and dates. No treatment-specific rules are applied.
:::

---

# Moorea backreef benthic community {#sec-moorea}

```{r load-percent-cover}
#| label: load-percent-cover

pc_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "PercentCoverData", "backreef_benthic_comp_mean_perc.csv"),
                    show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    benthic_group = factor(benthic_group,
      levels = c("coral", "ctb", "CCA", "macroalgae", "sand"),
      labels = c("Coral", "Rubble", "Crustose Coralline Algae", "Macroalgae", "Sand")),
    year = factor(year)
  )
```

## Figure

```{r fig-moorea-cover}
#| label: fig-moorea-cover
#| fig-cap: "Benthic community composition at the LTER1 Moorea backreef across three survey years. Stacked bars show mean percent cover for each benthic category. Colors match the community treatments used in this experiment: coral (orange), CCA/rubble (brown), and macroalgae (green)."
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 5

benthic_colors <- c(
  "Coral"                    = "#E07B54",
  "Crustose Coralline Algae" = "#A07850",
  "Macroalgae"               = "#2E8B57",
  "Rubble"                   = "#C8B89A",
  "Sand"                     = "#D6C8A8"
)

fig_moorea_cover <- ggplot(pc_data, aes(x = year, y = mean_percent_cover, fill = benthic_group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill", stat = "identity", color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(mean_percent_cover >= 3,
                               paste0(round(mean_percent_cover, 0), "%"), "")),
            position = position_fill(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white", size = 3.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = benthic_colors,
                    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent cover", fill = "Benthic category") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "right",
    legend.title       = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title         = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text          = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig0_moorea_percent_cover.png"),
       fig_moorea_cover, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_moorea_cover
```

---

# Biogeochemical differences by treatment {#sec-trt-diff}

Before testing the mediation pathway, we first ask whether the four community treatments produced measurable differences in NCP, NCC, pH, and DOC. Measurements are examined separately at **noon (12:00)** and **9 PM (21:00)** to distinguish daytime production and calcification signals from nighttime respiration dynamics. Mixed-effects models include tank identity as a random intercept to account for repeated daily measurements on the same tank (`response ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM)`).

## Figure

```{r fig-trt-diffs}
#| label: fig-trt-diffs
#| fig-cap: "Biogeochemical variables by community treatment at noon (left column) and 9 PM (right column). Points are individual tank means (n = 4 per treatment); crossbar shows the treatment mean. Compact letter display from Tukey HSD (p < 0.05); groups sharing a letter do not differ significantly. Dashed line at zero for NCP and NCC."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 11

library(multcomp)
library(multcompView)

trt_colors <- c("Control" = "#4682B4", "Algae_Dom" = "#2E8B57",
                "Coral_Dom" = "#E07B54", "Rubble_Dom" = "#A07850")
trt_labels <- c("Control" = "Control", "Algae_Dom" = "Macroalgae",
                "Coral_Dom" = "Coral",  "Rubble_Dom" = "CCA")

# ── Tank-level means ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# pH and DOC: from co-measured observations only (complete pairs)
complete_day_fig <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |> ungroup() |>
  filter(!is.na(pH), !is.na(DOC)) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
         TANK_NUM  = as.numeric(TANK_NUM))

complete_night_fig <- chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |>
  filter(!is.na(pH), !is.na(DOC)) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
         TANK_NUM  = as.numeric(TANK_NUM))

tank_avg_fig <- bind_rows(
  complete_day_fig   |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  complete_night_fig |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, Time) |>
  summarize(pH_mean  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
            DOC_mean = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(as.character(TANK_NUM)))

# NCP and NCC: full filtered datasets
tank_ncp_ncc_fig <- bind_rows(
  chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, Time) |>
  summarize(NCP_mean = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
            NCC_mean = mean(NEC, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

tank_all_fig <- tank_avg_fig |>
  left_join(tank_ncp_ncc_fig, by = c("TREATMENT", "TANK_NUM", "Time")) |>
  mutate(Time = factor(Time, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM")))

# ── Raw daily observations for violin backgrounds ──────────────────────────────
raw_ph_doc <- bind_rows(
  complete_day_fig   |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  complete_night_fig |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  rename(pH_mean = pH, DOC_mean = DOC) |>
  dplyr::select(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, DATE, Time, pH_mean, DOC_mean)

raw_ncp_ncc <- bind_rows(
  chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  rename(NCP_mean = NEP, NCC_mean = NEC) |>
  dplyr::select(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, DATE, Time, NCP_mean, NCC_mean) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(as.character(TANK_NUM)))

raw_all_fig <- raw_ph_doc |>
  full_join(raw_ncp_ncc, by = c("TREATMENT", "TANK_NUM", "DATE", "Time"),
            relationship = "many-to-many") |>
  distinct(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, DATE, Time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
    Time      = factor(Time, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM"))
  )

# ── Tukey letters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
get_letters_fig <- function(var, time_val, data, offset, raw_data = raw_all_fig) {
  df   <- data     |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  rdf  <- raw_data |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  mod  <- aov(reformulate("TREATMENT", response = var), data = df)
  ltrs <- multcompLetters4(mod, TukeyHSD(mod))$TREATMENT$Letters
  tibble(
    TREATMENT = factor(names(ltrs), levels = treatment_levels),
    letter    = ltrs,
    y_pos     = max(rdf[[var]], na.rm = TRUE) + offset,
    Variable  = var,
    Time      = factor(time_val, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM"))
  )
}

offsets <- list(NCP_mean=0.45, NCC_mean=0.12, pH_mean=0.012, DOC_mean=28)
letters_fig <- map2_dfr(
  names(offsets), offsets,
  ~bind_rows(
    get_letters_fig(.x, "Noon", tank_all_fig, .y),
    get_letters_fig(.x, "9 PM", tank_all_fig, .y)
  )
)

# ── Panel function ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_tank_panel_fig <- function(var, time_val, ylab, hline = FALSE,
                                show_x = FALSE, col_title = NULL) {
  df  <- tank_all_fig  |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  rdf <- raw_all_fig   |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  ldf <- letters_fig   |> filter(Variable == var,  Time == time_val)
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[var]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    { if (hline) geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey55",
                             linewidth = 0.4) } +
    geom_violin(data = rdf,
                aes(fill = TREATMENT),
                alpha = 0.12, color = NA, trim = FALSE) +
    geom_point(size = 3.2, alpha = 0.85) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "crossbar",
                 width = 0.42, linewidth = 0.65) +
    geom_text(data = ldf,
              aes(x = TREATMENT, y = y_pos, label = letter),
              color = "grey20", size = 3.2, fontface = "bold",
              inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab, title = col_title) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title      = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11),
          axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 9))
  if (show_x) p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 8))
  else        p + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
}

ncp_lab <- expression("NCP  (mmol C m"^{-2}~h^{-1}*")")
ncc_lab <- expression("NCC  (mmol CaCO"[3]~m^{-2}~h^{-1}*")")
ph_lab  <- expression("Mean pH"[T])
doc_lab <- expression("Mean DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^{-1}*")")

# Also keep make_trt_panel and per-variable plots available for downstream code
chem_plot_day   <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))
chem_plot_night <- chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

make_trt_panel <- function(df, var, ylab, hline = FALSE,
                           col_title = NULL, show_x = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[var]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    { if (hline) geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey50") } +
    geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.2,
               position = position_jitter(width = 0.08, seed = 7)) +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, geom = "errorbar",
                 fun.args = list(mult = 1), width = 0.22, linewidth = 0.8) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab, title = col_title) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12))
  if (show_x) p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
  else        p + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
}

ph_lab_orig  <- expression("pH"[T])
doc_lab_orig <- expression("DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^-1~")")

pd_ncp <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "NEP", ncp_lab, hline=TRUE, col_title="Noon (12:00)")
pd_ncc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "NEC", ncc_lab, hline=TRUE)
pd_ph  <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "pH",  ph_lab_orig)
pd_doc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "DOC", doc_lab_orig, show_x=TRUE) + scale_y_log10()
pn_ncp <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "NEP", ncp_lab, hline=TRUE, col_title="9 PM (21:00)")
pn_ncc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "NEC", ncc_lab, hline=TRUE)
pn_ph  <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "pH",  ph_lab_orig)
pn_doc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "DOC", doc_lab_orig, show_x=TRUE) + scale_y_log10()

# ── Primary figure: tank-level means with Tukey letters ──────────────────────
fig_trt <-
  (make_tank_panel_fig("NCP_mean","Noon",ncp_lab,hline=TRUE,col_title="Noon (12:00)") |
   make_tank_panel_fig("NCP_mean","9 PM",ncp_lab,hline=TRUE,col_title="9 PM (21:00)")) /
  (make_tank_panel_fig("NCC_mean","Noon",ncc_lab,hline=TRUE) |
   make_tank_panel_fig("NCC_mean","9 PM",ncc_lab,hline=TRUE)) /
  (make_tank_panel_fig("pH_mean", "Noon",ph_lab)  |
   make_tank_panel_fig("pH_mean", "9 PM",ph_lab)) /
  (make_tank_panel_fig("DOC_mean","Noon",doc_lab,show_x=TRUE) |
   make_tank_panel_fig("DOC_mean","9 PM",doc_lab,show_x=TRUE)) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    caption    = paste0(
      "Violin = distribution of all daily observations  |  ",
      "Points = individual tank means (n = 4 per treatment)  |  ",
      "Crossbar = treatment mean  |  ",
      "Letters = Tukey HSD (p < 0.05)"
    )
  ) &
  theme(plot.tag     = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 0))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig1_treatment_differences.png"),
       fig_trt, width = 8, height = 11, dpi = 300)

fig_trt
```

## Mixed-effects models

```{r trt-models}
#| label: trt-models

prep_model_data <- function(df) {
  df |> ungroup() |>
    mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
           TANK_NUM  = factor(TANK_NUM))
}

model_data_day   <- prep_model_data(chem_reframe_DAY_clean)
model_data_night <- prep_model_data(chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean)

fit_models <- function(df) {
  list(
    NCP = lmer(NEP ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE),
    NCC = lmer(NEC ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE),
    pH  = lmer(pH  ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE),
    # log(DOC): right-skewed distribution; log-transformation normalises
    # residuals and stabilises variance across treatment groups
    # REML = TRUE: restricted maximum likelihood gives unbiased variance
    # component estimates when the goal is inference on fixed effects
    DOC = lmer(log(DOC) ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE)
  )
}

mods_day   <- fit_models(model_data_day)
mods_night <- fit_models(model_data_night)

build_f_table <- function(mods, time_label) {
  map2(mods, names(mods),
       ~anova(.x) |> as.data.frame() |> mutate(Variable = .y, Time = time_label)) |>
    bind_rows() |>
    dplyr::select(Time, Variable, NumDF, DenDF, `F value`, `Pr(>F)`) |>
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))
}

f_table <- bind_rows(
  build_f_table(mods_day,   "Noon"),
  build_f_table(mods_night, "9 PM")
)

f_table |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Treatment effects at noon and 9 PM (Type III Satterthwaite F-tests). Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Time", "Variable", "df (num.)", "df (den.)", "F", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(f_table$`Pr(>F)` <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(f_table$`Pr(>F)` >= 0.05 & f_table$`Pr(>F)` < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
```

### Noon — pairwise contrasts

```{r trt-posthoc-day}
#| label: trt-posthoc-day

emm_day <- map2(
  mods_day, names(mods_day),
  ~emmeans(.x, pairwise ~ TREATMENT, adjust = "Tukey")$contrasts |>
    as.data.frame() |> mutate(Variable = .y)
) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  dplyr::select(Variable, contrast, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))

emm_day |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Noon: pairwise Tukey contrasts. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Variable", "Contrast", "Estimate", "SE", "df", "t", "p (adj.)")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_day$p.value <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_day$p.value >= 0.05 & emm_day$p.value < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
```

### 9 PM — pairwise contrasts

```{r trt-posthoc-night}
#| label: trt-posthoc-night

emm_night <- map2(
  mods_night, names(mods_night),
  ~emmeans(.x, pairwise ~ TREATMENT, adjust = "Tukey")$contrasts |>
    as.data.frame() |> mutate(Variable = .y)
) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  dplyr::select(Variable, contrast, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))

emm_night |>
  kbl(
    caption = "9 PM: pairwise Tukey contrasts. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Variable", "Contrast", "Estimate", "SE", "df", "t", "p (adj.)")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_night$p.value <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_night$p.value >= 0.05 & emm_night$p.value < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
```

## Interpretation

**At noon, NCP, NCC, and pH all differed significantly by treatment; DOC did not.** All three live-community treatments had higher daytime NCP than controls (Tukey p ≤ 0.04), but did not differ from each other — consistent with active benthic photosynthesis across all reef community types. The higher daytime production drove elevated pH in all three vs. control (p ≤ 0.009). Coral-enriched tanks had the highest NCC, differing significantly from both control (p = 0.015) and macroalgae (p = 0.009) treatments. DOC was not significantly different by treatment at noon (F = 1.6, p = 0.20).
