**At 9 PM, the treatment signal narrows.** Nighttime NCP did not differ by treatment (F = 0.97, p = 0.41) — all communities respire at broadly similar rates regardless of daytime production, erasing the production-driven biogeochemical differentiation. pH differences persisted into the night (F = 17.6, p < 0.001), reflecting structural chemical differences among tank types that are not simply driven by instantaneous photosynthesis. NCC remained marginally differentiated (F = 4.4, p = 0.027), with coral-enriched tanks again showing the highest nighttime calcification. DOC showed no treatment effect at night (F = 1.4, p = 0.29).

The contrast between noon NCP (significant) and 9 PM NCP (non-significant) supports using daytime-only measurements in the path analysis: the biogeochemical signal linking community production to water chemistry is a daytime phenomenon. Nighttime pH and NCC differences, while detectable, likely reflect carryover from daytime metabolic activity rather than active nighttime differentiation.

## NCP, NCC, pH, and DOC relationships

Having established that treatments differ in NCP and NCC, we now examine whether NCP covaries continuously with pH and DOC — and whether pH and NCC covary with DOC — across individual tank × date observations. Data from noon and 9 PM are combined (extreme NEP values > 4 mmol C m⁻² h⁻¹ excluded). Because measurements are nested within tanks, mixed-effects models are used with a random intercept for tank identity (`response ~ predictor * TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM)`). The treatment interaction term tests whether the slope between the two biogeochemical variables differs among community types.

```{r fig-ncp-relationships}
#| label: fig-ncp-relationships
#| fig-cap: "Relationships between biogeochemical variables across all noon and 9 PM observations. Points colored by community treatment. Lines are per-treatment OLS regressions; shaded bands are 95% CIs. Pooled R² and p-value shown in each panel."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10

library(ggpubr)

clean_chem_fig <- bind_rows(
  chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  mutate(
    # Remove NEP outliers using the same IQR × 3 criterion on the pooled diel data
    NEP       = remove_outliers(NEP),
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
    Time      = factor(Time, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM"))
  ) |>
  drop_na(NEP)

p_ncp_ph <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                   aes(x = NEP, y = pH)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65, aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              #, aes(group = TREATMENT),
              se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression("NCP  (mmol C m"^-2~h^-1~")"),
       y = expression("pH"[T])) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

p_ncp_doc <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                    aes(x = NEP, y = DOC)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65,
             aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = expression("NCP  (mmol C m"^-2~h^-1~")"),
       y = expression("DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^-1~")")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

p_ph_ncc <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                   aes(x = pH, y = NEC)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65, aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
              se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression("pH"[T]),
       y = expression("NCC  (mmol CaCO"[3]~m^-2~h^-1~")")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

p_ncc_doc <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                    aes(x = NEC, y = DOC)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65, aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
 # geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
  #            se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = expression("NCC  (mmol CaCO"[3]~m^-2~h^-1~")"),
       y = expression("DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^-1~")")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

fig_biogeo <- (p_ncp_ph | p_ncp_doc) /
              (p_ph_ncc | p_ncc_doc) +
                plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
                theme(legend.position = "bottom",
                      legend.text = element_text(size = 10))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig2_biogeochem_relationships.png"),
       fig_biogeo, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

fig_biogeo
```

```{r mod-ncp-relationships}
#| label: mod-ncp-relationships

mod_ncp_ph  <- lmer(pH  ~ NEP  + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)
mod_ncp_doc <- lmer(log(DOC) ~ NEP  + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)
mod_ph_ncc  <- lmer(NEC ~ pH + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)
mod_ncc_doc <- lmer(log(DOC) ~ NEC + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)

# Extract fixed-effect ANOVA tables (Type III F-tests on interaction term)
extract_anova <- function(mod, response) {
  anova(mod) |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Term") |>
    mutate(Response = response)
}

anova_table <- bind_rows(
  extract_anova(mod_ncp_ph,  "NCP → pH"),
  extract_anova(mod_ncp_doc, "NCP → DOC"),
  extract_anova(mod_ph_ncc,  "pH → NCC"),
  extract_anova(mod_ncc_doc, "NCC → DOC")
) |>
  dplyr::select(Response, Term, NumDF, DenDF, `F value`, `Pr(>F)`) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))

anova_table |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Type III F-tests (Satterthwaite) for mixed-effects models of biogeochemical relationships. Each model includes a treatment interaction and a random intercept for TANK_NUM. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Model", "Term", "df (num.)", "df (den.)", "F", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(anova_table$`Pr(>F)` <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(anova_table$`Pr(>F)` >= 0.05 & anova_table$`Pr(>F)` < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
```

**NCP is positively associated with pH**, consistent with photosynthetic CO₂ drawdown shifting the carbonate equilibrium toward higher pH. The treatment interaction term tests whether this slope differs among community types. **The NCP–DOC relationship** tests whether higher-production communities differ in dissolved organic carbon release, and whether that pattern is treatment-dependent. The two other panels examine **pH as a predictor of NCC** (whether more alkaline conditions support higher calcification rates) and **NCC as a predictor of DOC** (whether calcification co-varies with organic carbon dynamics across the diel cycle and among treatments). All four relationships are expected to align with the causal structure tested in the path analysis.

---

# Path model {#sec-path}


## Overview

Tank-level means are the unit of analysis (n = 16 independent tanks), avoiding within-tank pseudoreplication. All chemistry variables (NCP, pH, DOC) are derived from **daytime (12:00) measurements only**, consistent with the window in which NCP is measurable (daytime photosynthetic flux). All continuous variables are z-scored so path coefficients are directly comparable standardised β.

The model tests the full proposed causal chain from community metabolism to coral physiology:

```
NCP → pH, DOC
pH + DOC → Chl-a
Chl-a + DOC → Endosymbiont density
Chl-a + Endosymbiont → GP
Chl-a + GP → R
Endosymbiont + R → Tissue biomass
```

The structure separates two biologically distinct mediating variables: **Chl-a** as an index of per-cell light-harvesting capacity, and **endosymbiont density** as an index of symbiotic algal load. DOC enters as a direct predictor of endosymbiont density — an antagonistic community signal, supported by d-separation tests from a fragment-level piecewiseSEM, that bypasses the chlorophyll pathway and operates in parallel with the facilitative pH chain.

---

## Data preparation

```{r revised-tank-means}
#| label: revised-tank-means

scale_vec <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

# Tank-level physiology means (averaged across fragments) joined with
# daytime-only pipeline chemistry (outlier-filtered, daytime 12:00 only)
tank_means <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
  summarize(
    log_chla    = mean(log(chla_ug_cm2),        na.rm = TRUE),
    log_endo    = mean(log(endo_per_cm2),        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP     = mean(GP,                       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R      = mean(R,                        na.rm = TRUE),
    log_biomass = mean(log(mean_tissue_biomass), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP,              na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = max(pH,                na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(mean(DOC,          na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_chla, log_endo, mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

cat("n tanks:", nrow(tank_means), "\n")
```

---

## Path model

```{r revised-path-model}
#| label: revised-path-model

tank_v6_model <- "
  # NCP → water chemistry
  pH_tank  ~ a1*NEP_tank
  DOC_tank ~ a2*NEP_tank

  # Water chemistry → Chl-a
  log_chla ~ b1*pH_tank + b2*DOC_tank

  # Chl-a + DOC → endosymbiont density
  log_endo ~ c1*log_chla + c2*DOC_tank

  # Downstream physiology
  mean_GP     ~ d1*log_chla + d2*log_endo
  mean_R      ~ e1*log_chla + e2*mean_GP
  log_biomass ~ f1*log_endo + f2*mean_R
"

tank_v6_fit <- sem(tank_v6_model, data = as.data.frame(tank_means), estimator = "ML")
cat("Converged:", lavInspect(tank_v6_fit, "converged"), "\n")
```

## Direct path coefficients

```{r revised-path-coefs}
#| label: revised-path-coefs

revised_coefs <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(
    lhs = recode(lhs,
      pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC (log)", log_chla="Chl-a",
      log_endo="Endosymbiont", mean_GP="Gross photosynthesis",
      mean_R="Respiration", log_biomass="Tissue biomass"),
    rhs = recode(rhs,
      NEP_tank="NCP", pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC (log)",
      log_chla="Chl-a", log_endo="Endosymbiont",
      mean_GP="Gross photosynthesis", mean_R="Respiration"),
    across(c(est, se, std.all, pvalue), ~round(., 4))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Response = lhs, Predictor = rhs, Estimate = est, SE = se,
         z, `p-value` = pvalue, `Std. β` = std.all)

revised_coefs |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Revised model direct path coefficients (n = ", nrow(tank_means), " tanks). ",
      "All variables z-scored. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Estimate", "SE", "z", "p", "Std. \u03b2")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(revised_coefs$`p-value` <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(revised_coefs$`p-value` >= 0.05 & revised_coefs$`p-value` < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
```

## R² for endogenous variables

```{r revised-path-r2}
#| label: revised-path-r2

lavInspect(tank_v6_fit, "r2") |>
  tibble::enframe(name = "Variable", value = "R2") |>
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(Variable,
      pH_tank     = "pH",
      DOC_tank    = "DOC (log)",
      log_chla    = "Chl-a",
      log_endo    = "Endosymbiont density",
      mean_GP     = "Gross photosynthesis",
      mean_R      = "Respiration",
      log_biomass = "Tissue biomass"
    ),
    R2 = round(R2, 3)
  ) |>
  kbl(
    caption = "R² for each endogenous variable in the main path model. Values represent the proportion of variance explained by all predictors in the model for each response variable.",
    col.names = c("Variable", "R²")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

## Path diagram

```{r fig-path-diagram}
#| label: fig-path-diagram
#| fig-cap: "Revised path diagram (tank-level SEM, n = 16 tanks). Green = significant positive path (p < 0.05); red = significant negative path (p < 0.05); grey = n.s. Coefficients are standardised β."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5

library(purrr)

shorten_arrow <- function(x0, y0, x1, y1, d0 = 0.48, d1 = 0.48) {
  dx <- x1 - x0; dy <- y1 - y0; d <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2)
  tibble(xs = x0 + dx/d*d0, ys = y0 + dy/d*d0,
         xe = x1 - dx/d*d1, ye = y1 - dy/d*d1,
         mx = (x0 + dx/d*d0 + x1 - dx/d*d1) / 2,
         my = (y0 + dy/d*d0 + y1 - dy/d*d1) / 2)
}

revised_nodes <- tibble(
  id    = c("NCP",  "pH",  "DOC",  "Chla",  "Endo",                  "GP",                    "R",           "Bio"),
  label = c("NCP",  "pH",  "DOC",  "Chl-a", "Endosymbiont\ndensity", "Gross\nphotosynthesis", "Respiration", "Tissue\nbiomass"),
  x     = c(1,       3,     3,      5.5,     7.5,                     10,                      10,            10),
  y     = c(0,       1.2,  -1.2,    0,       0,                       1.8,                     0,            -1.8)
)

# Add R² from model to each endogenous node
r2_v6 <- lavInspect(tank_v6_fit, "r2")
revised_nodes <- revised_nodes |>
  left_join(
    tibble(id = c("pH",     "DOC",      "Chla",     "Endo",     "GP",       "R",      "Bio"),
           r2 = r2_v6[c("pH_tank","DOC_tank","log_chla","log_endo","mean_GP","mean_R","log_biomass")]),
    by = "id"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    r2_label = ifelse(!is.na(r2), sprintf("R\u00b2=%.2f", r2), NA_character_),
    label_y  = ifelse(!is.na(r2), y + 0.07, y)
  )

revised_edge_coords <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(
    from = recode(rhs, NEP_tank="NCP", pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC",
                  log_chla="Chla", log_endo="Endo", mean_GP="GP", mean_R="R"),
    to   = recode(lhs, pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC", log_chla="Chla",
                  log_endo="Endo", mean_GP="GP", mean_R="R", log_biomass="Bio"),
    colour = case_when(
      pvalue < 0.05 & std.all > 0 ~ "pos",
      pvalue < 0.05 & std.all < 0 ~ "neg",
      TRUE                         ~ "ns"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(from, to, coef = std.all, colour) |>
  left_join(revised_nodes |> dplyr::select(id, x, y), by = c("from" = "id")) |> rename(x0=x, y0=y) |>
  left_join(revised_nodes |> dplyr::select(id, x, y), by = c("to"   = "id")) |> rename(x1=x, y1=y) |>
  pmap_dfr(function(from, to, coef, colour, x0, y0, x1, y1) {
    pts <- shorten_arrow(x0, y0, x1, y1)
    tibble(from, to, coef, colour,
           xs=pts$xs, ys=pts$ys, xe=pts$xe, ye=pts$ye, mx=pts$mx, my=pts$my)
  })

colour_vals <- c("pos" = "#2ca25f", "neg" = "#e34a33", "ns" = "grey70")

fig_revised_path <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = revised_edge_coords,
    aes(x=xs, y=ys, xend=xe, yend=ye, color=colour),
    linewidth = 0.85,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = revised_edge_coords,
    aes(x=mx, y=my, label=sprintf("%.2f", coef), color=colour),
    size = 2.5, label.padding = unit(0.10, "lines"), label.size = 0,
    fill = "white", show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_tile(
    data = revised_nodes, aes(x=x, y=y),
    width = 0.88, height = 0.56,
    fill = "white", color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = revised_nodes, aes(x=x, y=label_y, label=label),
    size = 2.7, lineheight = 0.88
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = revised_nodes |> filter(!is.na(r2_label)),
    aes(x=x, y=y-0.13, label=r2_label),
    size = 2.1, color = "grey40", fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colour_vals) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.8, y = -2.6,
           label = "Green = sig. positive   Red = sig. negative   Grey = n.s.   Standardised \u03b2   n = 16 tanks",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.6, color = "grey35") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0.6, 10.7), ylim = c(-2.7, 2.4), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig_revised_path_diagram.png"),
       fig_revised_path, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_revised_path
```

## Global model fit

```{r revised-path-fit}
#| label: revised-path-fit

fit_revised <- fitMeasures(tank_v6_fit,
                           c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "rmsea", "srmr"))
data.frame(
  Statistic = names(fit_revised),
  Value     = round(as.numeric(fit_revised), 4)
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Revised model global fit. CFI > 0.90, RMSEA < 0.08, p(\u03c7\u00b2) > 0.05 indicate acceptable fit.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Model fit caveat:** CFI (0.85) and RMSEA (0.17) fall below conventional thresholds. At n = 16 tanks the χ²-based fit statistics are unreliable; the non-significant model χ² (p = 0.11) and the low χ²/df ratio (23.2/16 = 1.45) are the more relevant indicators at this sample size. Interpret fit indices cautiously and focus on individual path coefficients and their directions.
:::

---

## Indirect effects

```{r revised-indirect}
#| label: revised-indirect

# Re-fit model with indirect effects defined via := operator
tank_v6_indirect_model <- "
  # NCP → water chemistry
  pH_tank  ~ a1*NEP_tank
  DOC_tank ~ a2*NEP_tank

  # Water chemistry → Chl-a
  log_chla ~ b1*pH_tank + b2*DOC_tank

  # Chl-a + DOC → endosymbiont density
  log_endo ~ c1*log_chla + c2*DOC_tank

  # Downstream physiology
  mean_GP     ~ d1*log_chla + d2*log_endo
  mean_R      ~ e1*log_chla + e2*mean_GP
  log_biomass ~ f1*log_endo + f2*mean_R

  # ── Indirect effects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Facilitative pH chain
  ind_NCP_Chla          := a1*b1
  ind_NCP_Endo_pH       := a1*b1*c1
  ind_NCP_GP_pH         := a1*b1*d1 + a1*b1*c1*d2
  ind_NCP_R_pH          := (a1*b1*d1 + a1*b1*c1*d2)*e2
  ind_NCP_Bio_pH        := a1*b1*c1*f1

  # Antagonistic DOC chain
  ind_DOC_Endo_via_Chla := b2*c1
  ind_DOC_Bio_total     := (c2 + b2*c1)*f1

  # NCP → DOC → downstream
  ind_NCP_Endo_DOC      := a2*(c2 + b2*c1)

  # Total NCP indirect effects on key endpoints
  tot_NCP_Endo          := a1*b1*c1 + a2*(c2 + b2*c1)
  tot_NCP_Bio           := a1*b1*c1*f1 + a2*(c2 + b2*c1)*f1
"

tank_v6_indirect_fit <- sem(tank_v6_indirect_model,
                            data      = as.data.frame(tank_means),
                            estimator = "ML")

indirect_tbl <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_indirect_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == ":=") |>
  mutate(
    Path = recode(lhs,
      ind_NCP_Chla          = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a",
      ind_NCP_Endo_pH       = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a \u2192 Endo",
      ind_NCP_GP_pH         = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a \u2194 Endo \u2192 GP",
      ind_NCP_R_pH          = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 \u2026 \u2192 GP \u2192 R",
      ind_NCP_Bio_pH        = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a \u2192 Endo \u2192 Biomass",
      ind_DOC_Endo_via_Chla = "DOC \u2192 Chl-a \u2192 Endo",
      ind_DOC_Bio_total     = "DOC \u2192 Endo \u2192 Biomass (all routes)",
      ind_NCP_Endo_DOC      = "NCP \u2192 DOC \u2192 Endo (all routes)",
      tot_NCP_Endo          = "NCP total \u2192 Endo",
      tot_NCP_Bio           = "NCP total \u2192 Biomass"
    ),
    across(c(est, se, std.all, pvalue), ~round(., 4))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Path, std_beta = std.all, SE = se, z, pval = pvalue)

indirect_tbl |>
  kbl(
    col.names = c("Path", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "z", "p-value"),
    caption = paste0(
      "Indirect (mediated) effects from the revised tank-level SEM (n = 16 tanks). ",
      "Std. \u03b2 from delta method; p-values are two-tailed Wald tests. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    )
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_tbl$pval <  0.05),
           background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_tbl$pval >= 0.05 & indirect_tbl$pval < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
```

---

## Path scatter plots

```{r revised-scatter-data}
#| label: revised-scatter-data

# Tank means with TREATMENT — original (unlogged) values for all variables
tank_means_raw <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarize(
    chla    = mean(chla_ug_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    endo    = mean(endo_per_cm2,       na.rm = TRUE),
    gp      = mean(GP,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    r       = mean(R,                   na.rm = TRUE),
    biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = max(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(nep) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Fragment-level physiology (original values) joined to tank chemistry means
raw_frag_path <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
         chla    = chla_ug_cm2,
         endo    = endo_per_cm2,
         biomass = mean_tissue_biomass,
         gp      = GP,
         r       = R) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = max(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Daily chemistry (original values) for chemistry-chemistry panels
raw_chem_path <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |> ungroup() |>
  rename(nep = NEP, ph = pH, doc = DOC) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Path coefficients for panel annotations and significance check
path_coefs_v6 <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_indirect_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  dplyr::select(lhs, rhs, std.all, pvalue)

get_beta_label <- function(response, predictor) {
  row <- path_coefs_v6 |> filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor)
  if (nrow(row) == 0) return("")
  p_str <- if (row$pvalue < 0.001) "p < 0.001" else sprintf("p = %.3f", row$pvalue)
  sprintf("\u03b2 = %.2f, %s", row$std.all, p_str)
}

is_sig <- function(response, predictor, alpha = 0.05) {
  path_coefs_v6 |>
    filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor) |>
    pull(pvalue) < alpha
}

# Scatter: grey raw pts + conditional lm line + coloured tank means + beta annotation
# log_x / log_y: display axis on log10 scale (data passed as original values)
# response_var / predictor_var: SEM variable names used for coefficient lookup
scatter_path_v6 <- function(x, y, xlab, ylab,
                            response_var, predictor_var, raw_data,
                            log_x = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  beta_lbl   <- get_beta_label(response_var, predictor_var)
  sig        <- is_sig(response_var, predictor_var)
  main_clean <- tank_means_raw |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))
  raw_clean  <- raw_data       |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))

  # x = -Inf is invalid on log10 axes; use actual data minimum instead
  x_ann <- if (log_x) min(main_clean[[x]], na.rm = TRUE) else -Inf

  p <- ggplot(main_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]])) +
    geom_point(data = raw_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]]),
               color = "grey60", alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    { if (sig) geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "grey30", fill = "grey80",
                           linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.25, se = TRUE) } +
    geom_point(aes(fill = TREATMENT), shape = 21, size = 3.2,
               alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0.4, color = "grey20") +
    annotate("text", x = x_ann, y = Inf, label = beta_lbl,
             hjust = -0.08, vjust = 1.5, size = 3.0,
             color = "grey25", fontface = "italic") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title = element_text(size = 9))

  if (log_x) p <- p + scale_x_log10()
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}
```

```{r fig-path-scatters}
#| label: fig-path-scatters
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 18
#| fig-cap: "Bivariate scatter plots for each direct path in the revised SEM. Grey points show raw observations (daily chemistry or fragment-level physiology). Coloured circles show tank means (n = 16), filled by treatment. Regression line shown only for significant paths (p < 0.05); axes for log-transformed SEM variables displayed on original scale with log\u2081\u2080 spacing. Annotated \u03b2 is the standardised path coefficient."

xncp  <- expression("NCP (mmol C m"^-2~h^-1*")")
xph   <- expression("Max pH"[T])
xdoc  <- expression("DOC (\u03bcmol L"^-1*")")
xchla <- expression("Chl-a (\u03bcg cm"^-2*")")
xendo <- expression("Endosymbiont density ("*10^6~"cells cm"^-2*")")
xgp   <- expression("Gross photosynthesis (\u03bcmol O"[2]~cm^-2~h^-1*")")
xr    <- expression("Respiration (\u03bcmol O"[2]~cm^-2~h^-1*")")
xbio  <- expression("Tissue biomass (mg cm"^-2*")")

#                   x       y         xlab   ylab   response       predictor     raw_data      log_x  log_y
s1  <- scatter_path_v6("nep","ph",      xncp,  xph,  "pH_tank",    "NEP_tank",  raw_chem_path, FALSE, FALSE)
s2  <- scatter_path_v6("nep","doc",     xncp,  xdoc, "DOC_tank",   "NEP_tank",  raw_chem_path, FALSE, TRUE)
s3  <- scatter_path_v6("ph","chla",     xph,   xchla,"log_chla",   "pH_tank",   raw_frag_path, FALSE, TRUE)
s4  <- scatter_path_v6("doc","chla",    xdoc,  xchla,"log_chla",   "DOC_tank",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s5  <- scatter_path_v6("chla","endo",   xchla, xendo,"log_endo",   "log_chla",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s6  <- scatter_path_v6("doc","endo",    xdoc,  xendo,"log_endo",   "DOC_tank",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s7  <- scatter_path_v6("chla","gp",     xchla, xgp,  "mean_GP",    "log_chla",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  FALSE)
s8  <- scatter_path_v6("endo","gp",     xendo, xgp,  "mean_GP",    "log_endo",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  FALSE)
s9  <- scatter_path_v6("gp","r",        xgp,   xr,   "mean_R",     "mean_GP",   raw_frag_path, FALSE, FALSE)
s10 <- scatter_path_v6("chla","r",      xchla, xr,   "mean_R",     "log_chla",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  FALSE)
s11 <- scatter_path_v6("endo","biomass",xendo, xbio, "log_biomass","log_endo",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s12 <- scatter_path_v6("r","biomass",   xr,    xbio, "log_biomass","mean_R",    raw_frag_path, FALSE, TRUE)

fig_path_scatters <-
  (s1  | s2)  /
  (s3  | s4)  /
  (s5  | s6)  /
  (s7  | s8)  /
  (s9  | s10) /
  (s11 | s12) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    caption    = paste0(
      "Grey points = raw observations (daily chemistry or fragment-level physiology)  |  ",
      "Coloured circles = tank means (n = 16)  |  ",
      "\u03b2 = standardised path coefficient from revised SEM"
    )
  ) &
  theme(plot.tag     = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 0))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig_revised_path_scatters.png"),
       fig_path_scatters, width = 8, height = 18, dpi = 300)

fig_path_scatters
```

---

## Interpretation

**The facilitative chain.** NCP is positively associated with pH (β = 0.65, p = 0.001), consistent with photosynthetic CO₂ drawdown shifting the carbonate equilibrium. pH in turn predicts Chl-a in downstream corals (β = 0.49, p = 0.024), and Chl-a propagates through endosymbiont density (β = 0.45, p = 0.010), which predicts tissue biomass (β = 0.73, p < 0.001). This is the proposed material-legacy facilitative pathway: community photosynthesis → carbonate chemistry → symbiont photophysiology → coral growth.

**The antagonistic DOC route.** DOC has a significant direct negative effect on endosymbiont density (β = −0.50, p = 0.005) that bypasses Chl-a entirely. Higher-DOC tanks are associated with lower symbiont cell densities in downstream corals regardless of their pigment content, suggesting that macroalgal-dominated communities carry an antagonistic community legacy that operates in parallel with the facilitative pH chain. NCP → DOC is non-significant (β = 0.17, p = 0.50), so the source of tank-to-tank DOC variation is not fully captured by the model's upstream structure.

**Gross photosynthesis and respiration.** Neither Chl-a (β = 0.18, p = 0.52) nor endosymbiont density (β = 0.26, p = 0.34) significantly predicts gross photosynthesis at the tank level. Respiration is predicted by both Chl-a (β = 0.47, p = 0.002) and gross photosynthesis (β = 0.55, p < 0.001), indicating respiratory demand scales with both symbiont pigment content and photosynthetic output. R → Tissue biomass is non-significant (β = 0.02, p = 0.91): tissue accrual is driven by endosymbiont cell density, not respiratory flux.

::: {.callout-note}
**Potential outliers: corals 53 and 114.** Fragments from coral 53 (genotype L) and coral 114 (genotype S) in tank 1 had endosymbiont densities in the bottom 5% of the entire dataset (~0.04 × 10⁶ cells cm⁻²), approximately 10-fold below the dataset median. These fragments may reflect localised bleaching or a sampling artefact and could exert leverage on tank-level path estimates. Sensitivity models excluding these two fragments produced path coefficients within |Δβ| ≤ 0.05 of the full model with all significance conclusions preserved, indicating that the primary results are not driven by these observations.
:::

---

# Sensitivity analysis: corals 53 & 114 excluded {#sec-sensitivity}

Fragments from coral 53 (genotype L) and coral 114 (genotype S) in tank 1 had
endosymbiont densities in the bottom 5% of the entire dataset (~0.04 cells cm⁻²),
approximately 10-fold below the dataset median. These two fragments inflate the
tank 1 Chl-a mean and could exert leverage on tank-level path estimates. This
section re-fits the primary path model after removing those two fragments from the
tank-level averages, to assess whether the main conclusions are robust to their
inclusion.

## Tank means — fragments 53 & 114 excluded

```{r sens-tank-means}
#| label: sens-tank-means

tank_means_excl <- metadata |>
  filter(!(CORAL_NUM %in% c(53, 114))) |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
  summarize(
    log_chla    = mean(log(chla_ug_cm2),        na.rm = TRUE),
    log_endo    = mean(log(endo_per_cm2),        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP     = mean(GP,                       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R      = mean(R,                        na.rm = TRUE),
    log_biomass = mean(log(mean_tissue_biomass), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP,        na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = max(pH,          na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(mean(DOC,    na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_chla, log_endo, mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

cat("n tanks:", nrow(tank_means_excl), "\n")
```

## Path model — fragments 53 & 114 excluded

```{r sens-path-model}
#| label: sens-path-model

tank_v6_fit_excl <- sem(tank_v6_model,
                        data      = as.data.frame(tank_means_excl),
                        estimator = "ML")

# ── Side-by-side coefficient comparison ──────────────────────────────────────
coefs_orig <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(label = paste(lhs, rhs, sep = " ~ ")) |>
  dplyr::select(label, beta_orig = std.all, p_orig = pvalue)

coefs_excl <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_fit_excl, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(label = paste(lhs, rhs, sep = " ~ ")) |>
  dplyr::select(label, beta_excl = std.all, p_excl = pvalue)

comparison_tbl <- coefs_orig |>
  left_join(coefs_excl, by = "label") |>
  mutate(
    Path = recode(label,
      "pH_tank ~ NEP_tank"     = "NCP \u2192 pH",
      "DOC_tank ~ NEP_tank"    = "NCP \u2192 DOC",
      "log_chla ~ pH_tank"     = "pH \u2192 Chl-a",
      "log_chla ~ DOC_tank"    = "DOC \u2192 Chl-a",
      "log_endo ~ log_chla"    = "Chl-a \u2192 Endo",
      "log_endo ~ DOC_tank"    = "DOC \u2192 Endo",
      "mean_GP ~ log_chla"     = "Chl-a \u2192 GP",
      "mean_GP ~ log_endo"     = "Endo \u2192 GP",
      "mean_R ~ log_chla"      = "Chl-a \u2192 R",
      "mean_R ~ mean_GP"       = "GP \u2192 R",
      "log_biomass ~ log_endo" = "Endo \u2192 Biomass",
      "log_biomass ~ mean_R"   = "R \u2192 Biomass"
    ),
    across(c(beta_orig, p_orig, beta_excl, p_excl), ~round(., 3)),
    delta_beta = round(beta_excl - beta_orig, 3)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Path,
                `β (full)` = beta_orig, `p (full)` = p_orig,
                `β (excl.)` = beta_excl, `p (excl.)` = p_excl,
                `Δβ` = delta_beta)

comparison_tbl |>
  kbl(caption = paste0(
    "Sensitivity analysis: standardised path coefficients from the full model ",
    "(n = 16 tanks, all fragments) versus the model with corals 53 and 114 removed. ",
    "\u0394\u03b2 = \u03b2(excl.) \u2212 \u03b2(full). ",
    "Green rows = significant (p < 0.05) in both versions."
  )) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE, position = "left") |>
  row_spec(
    which(comparison_tbl[["p (full)"]] < 0.05 & comparison_tbl[["p (excl.)"]] < 0.05),
    background = "#d4edda"
  )
```


::: {.callout-note}
**Note on potential outliers:** Fragments 53 and 114 showed unusually low endosymbiont
densities (bottom 5% of the dataset), possibly reflecting fragment-level bleaching or a
sampling artefact. They may be outliers worth investigating further, though the sensitivity
comparison table above shows that their inclusion does not materially alter the path
coefficients or significance conclusions.
:::

---

# Alternative model: Chl-a per endosymbiont {#sec-chl-per-endo}

This section re-runs the same structural path model, replacing Chl-a per cm² with
**Chl-a per endosymbiont cell** (µg chl cell⁻¹) as the pigment variable.
This re-framing separates total pigment load (driven partly by cell density) from
per-cell pigment content, which is a more direct index of symbiont photophysiological
quality (e.g., bleaching state).  All other variables and the causal topology are
identical to the revised model above.

## Tank means — Chl-a per endosymbiont

```{r cpe-tank-means}
#| label: cpe-tank-means

tank_means_cpe <- metadata |>
  mutate(
    mean_tissue_biomass  = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
    chla_per_endo        = chla_ug_cm2 / endo_per_cm2   # µg chl cell⁻¹
  ) |>
  #filter(chla_per_endo < 20) |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
  summarize(
    log_chla_per_endo = mean(log(chla_per_endo),         na.rm = TRUE),
    log_endo          = mean(log(endo_per_cm2),           na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP           = mean(GP,                          na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R            = mean(R,                           na.rm = TRUE),
    log_biomass       = mean(log(mean_tissue_biomass),    na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP,       na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = max(pH,         na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(max(DOC,   na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_chla_per_endo, log_endo,
                  mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

cat("n tanks:", nrow(tank_means_cpe), "\n")
```

## Path model — Chl-a per endosymbiont

```{r cpe-path-model}
#| label: cpe-path-model

cpe_model <- "
  # NCP → water chemistry
  pH_tank  ~ a1*NEP_tank
  DOC_tank ~ a2*NEP_tank

  # Water chemistry → Chl-a per cell
  log_chla_per_endo ~ b1*pH_tank + b2*DOC_tank

  # Chl-a per cell → endosymbiont density
  log_endo ~ c1*log_chla_per_endo

  # Endo → Biomass → R → GP (with direct Endo effects on R and GP)
  log_biomass ~ f1*log_endo
  mean_R      ~ e1*log_biomass + e2*log_endo
  mean_GP     ~ d1*mean_R      + d2*log_endo
"

cpe_fit <- sem(cpe_model,
               data      = as.data.frame(tank_means_cpe),
               estimator = "ML")

# Direct path table
parameterEstimates(cpe_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(
    from = recode(rhs,
      NEP_tank          = "NCP",
      pH_tank           = "pH",
      DOC_tank          = "DOC (log)",
      log_chla_per_endo = "Chl-a per cell",
      log_endo          = "Endosymbiont density",
      log_biomass       = "Tissue biomass",
      mean_R            = "Respiration"
    ),
    to = recode(lhs,
      pH_tank           = "pH",
      DOC_tank          = "DOC (log)",
      log_chla_per_endo = "Chl-a per cell",
      log_endo          = "Endosymbiont density",
      log_biomass       = "Tissue biomass",
      mean_R            = "Respiration",
      mean_GP           = "Gross photosynthesis"
    ),
    across(c(est, se, std.all, pvalue), ~round(., 4))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(From = from, To = to, std_beta = std.all, SE = se, z, pval = pvalue) |>
  kbl(
    col.names = c("From", "To", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "z", "p-value"),
    caption   = paste0(
      "Chl-a-per-endosymbiont model: direct path coefficients (n = ",
      nrow(tank_means_cpe), " tanks). "
    )
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE, position = "left") |>
  row_spec(which(
    parameterEstimates(cpe_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
      filter(op == "~") |> pull(pvalue) < 0.05
  ), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(
    parameterEstimates(cpe_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
      filter(op == "~") |>
      mutate(q = pvalue >= 0.05 & pvalue < 0.10) |>
      pull(q)
  ), background = "#fff3cd")
```

## R² for endogenous variables — Chl-a per endosymbiont

```{r cpe-path-r2}
#| label: cpe-path-r2

lavInspect(cpe_fit, "r2") |>
  tibble::enframe(name = "Variable", value = "R2") |>
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(Variable,
      pH_tank           = "pH",
      DOC_tank          = "DOC (log)",
      log_chla_per_endo = "Chl-a per cell",
      log_endo          = "Endosymbiont density",
      mean_GP           = "Gross photosynthesis",
      mean_R            = "Respiration",
      log_biomass       = "Tissue biomass"
    ),
    R2 = round(R2, 3)
  ) |>
  kbl(
    caption = "R² for each endogenous variable in the Chl-a-per-endosymbiont path model. Values represent the proportion of variance explained by all predictors for each response variable.",
    col.names = c("Variable", "R²")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

## Global model fit — Chl-a per endosymbiont

```{r cpe-fit-measures}
#| label: cpe-fit-measures

fit_cpe <- fitMeasures(cpe_fit,
                       c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "rmsea", "srmr"))
data.frame(
  Statistic = names(fit_cpe),
  Value     = round(as.numeric(fit_cpe), 4)
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Chl-a-per-endosymbiont model global fit.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

## Indirect effects — Chl-a per endosymbiont

```{r cpe-indirect}
#| label: cpe-indirect

cpe_indirect_model <- "
  # NCP → water chemistry
  pH_tank  ~ a1*NEP_tank
  DOC_tank ~ a2*NEP_tank

  # Water chemistry → Chl-a per cell
  log_chla_per_endo ~ b1*pH_tank + b2*DOC_tank

  # Chl-a per cell → endosymbiont density
  log_endo ~ c1*log_chla_per_endo

  # Endo → Biomass → R → GP (with direct Endo effects on R and GP)
  log_biomass ~ f1*log_endo
  mean_R      ~ e1*log_biomass + e2*log_endo
  mean_GP     ~ d1*mean_R      + d2*log_endo

  # ── Indirect effects ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # NCP → Chl/cell (via pH and DOC)
  ind_NCP_ChlCell_pH   := a1*b1
  ind_NCP_ChlCell_DOC  := a2*b2

  # NCP → Endo (via Chl/cell)
  ind_NCP_Endo_pH      := a1*b1*c1
  ind_NCP_Endo_DOC     := a2*b2*c1
  tot_NCP_Endo         := (a1*b1 + a2*b2)*c1

  # NCP → Biomass (via Chl/cell → Endo → Bio)
  tot_NCP_Bio          := (a1*b1 + a2*b2)*c1*f1

  # NCP → R: via Bio→R (e1) and direct Endo→R (e2)
  tot_NCP_R            := (a1*b1 + a2*b2)*c1*(f1*e1 + e2)

  # NCP → GP: via R (d1) and direct Endo→GP (d2)
  tot_NCP_GP           := (a1*b1 + a2*b2)*c1*((f1*e1 + e2)*d1 + d2)

  # DOC → downstream via Chl/cell
  ind_DOC_Endo         := b2*c1
  ind_DOC_Bio          := b2*c1*f1
  ind_DOC_R            := b2*c1*(f1*e1 + e2)
  ind_DOC_GP           := b2*c1*((f1*e1 + e2)*d1 + d2)
"

cpe_indirect_fit <- sem(cpe_indirect_model,
                        data      = as.data.frame(tank_means_cpe),
                        estimator = "ML")

indirect_tbl_cpe <- parameterEstimates(cpe_indirect_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == ":=") |>
  mutate(
    Path = recode(lhs,
      ind_NCP_ChlCell_pH  = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a/cell",
      ind_NCP_ChlCell_DOC = "NCP \u2192 DOC \u2192 Chl-a/cell",
      ind_NCP_Endo_pH     = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a/cell \u2192 Endo",
      ind_NCP_Endo_DOC    = "NCP \u2192 DOC \u2192 Chl-a/cell \u2192 Endo",
      tot_NCP_Endo        = "NCP total \u2192 Endo",
      tot_NCP_Bio         = "NCP total \u2192 Biomass",
      tot_NCP_R           = "NCP total \u2192 R",
      tot_NCP_GP          = "NCP total \u2192 GP",
      ind_DOC_Endo        = "DOC \u2192 Chl-a/cell \u2192 Endo",
      ind_DOC_Bio         = "DOC \u2192 \u2026 \u2192 Biomass",
      ind_DOC_R           = "DOC \u2192 \u2026 \u2192 R",
      ind_DOC_GP          = "DOC \u2192 \u2026 \u2192 GP"
    ),
    across(c(est, se, std.all, pvalue), ~round(., 4))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Path, std_beta = std.all, SE = se, z, pval = pvalue)

indirect_tbl_cpe |>
  kbl(
    col.names = c("Path", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "z", "p-value"),
    caption   = paste0(
      "Indirect (mediated) effects — Chl-a-per-endosymbiont model (n = ",
      nrow(tank_means_cpe), " tanks). ",
      "Std. \u03b2 from delta method; p-values are two-tailed Wald tests. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    )
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_tbl_cpe$pval <  0.05),  background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_tbl_cpe$pval >= 0.05 & indirect_tbl_cpe$pval < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
```

## Path diagram — Chl-a per endosymbiont

```{r cpe-path-diagram}
#| label: cpe-path-diagram
#| fig-cap: "Chl-a-per-endosymbiont model path diagram (n = 16 tanks). Green = significant positive path (p < 0.05); red = significant negative path (p < 0.05); grey = n.s. Coefficients are standardised β."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5

cpe_nodes <- tibble(
  id    = c("NCP", "pH",  "DOC",  "Chla",            "Endo",                  "Bio",             "R",           "GP"),
  label = c("NCP", "pH",  "DOC",  "Chl-a\nper cell", "Endosymbiont\ndensity", "Tissue\nbiomass", "Respiration", "Gross\nphotosynthesis"),
  x     = c(1,      3,     3,      5.5,                7.5,                     9.5,               9.5,           11.5),
  y     = c(0,      1.0,  -1.0,    0,                  0,                       1.0,              -0.8,            0.0)
)

# Add R² from model to each endogenous node
r2_cpe <- lavInspect(cpe_fit, "r2")
cpe_nodes <- cpe_nodes |>
  left_join(
    tibble(id = c("pH",     "DOC",      "Chla",              "Endo",     "Bio",        "R",      "GP"),
           r2 = r2_cpe[c("pH_tank","DOC_tank","log_chla_per_endo","log_endo","log_biomass","mean_R","mean_GP")]),
    by = "id"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    r2_label = ifelse(!is.na(r2), sprintf("R\u00b2=%.2f", r2), NA_character_),
    label_y  = ifelse(!is.na(r2), y + 0.07, y)
  )

cpe_edge_coords <- parameterEstimates(cpe_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(
    from = recode(rhs, NEP_tank="NCP", pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC",
                  log_chla_per_endo="Chla", log_endo="Endo",
                  log_biomass="Bio", mean_R="R"),
    to   = recode(lhs, pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC",
                  log_chla_per_endo="Chla", log_endo="Endo",
                  log_biomass="Bio", mean_R="R", mean_GP="GP"),
    colour = case_when(
      pvalue < 0.05 & std.all > 0 ~ "pos",
      pvalue < 0.05 & std.all < 0 ~ "neg",
      TRUE                         ~ "ns"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(from, to, coef = std.all, colour) |>
  left_join(cpe_nodes |> dplyr::select(id, x, y), by = c("from" = "id")) |> rename(x0=x, y0=y) |>
  left_join(cpe_nodes |> dplyr::select(id, x, y), by = c("to"   = "id")) |> rename(x1=x, y1=y) |>
  pmap_dfr(function(from, to, coef, colour, x0, y0, x1, y1) {
    pts <- shorten_arrow(x0, y0, x1, y1)
    tibble(from, to, coef, colour,
           xs=pts$xs, ys=pts$ys, xe=pts$xe, ye=pts$ye, mx=pts$mx, my=pts$my)
  })

fig_cpe_path <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = cpe_edge_coords,
    aes(x=xs, y=ys, xend=xe, yend=ye, color=colour),
    linewidth = 0.85,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = cpe_edge_coords,
    aes(x=mx, y=my, label=sprintf("%.2f", coef), color=colour),
    size = 2.5, label.padding = unit(0.10, "lines"), label.size = 0,
    fill = "white", show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_tile(
    data = cpe_nodes, aes(x=x, y=y),
    width = 0.88, height = 0.56,
    fill = "white", color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = cpe_nodes, aes(x=x, y=label_y, label=label),
    size = 2.7, lineheight = 0.88
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = cpe_nodes |> filter(!is.na(r2_label)),
    aes(x=x, y=y-0.13, label=r2_label),
    size = 2.1, color = "grey40", fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colour_vals) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.8, y = -1.55,
           label = "Green = sig. positive   Red = sig. negative   Grey = n.s.   Standardised \u03b2   n = 16 tanks",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.6, color = "grey35") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0.6, 12.2), ylim = c(-1.8, 1.8), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig_cpe_path_diagram.png"),
       fig_cpe_path, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_cpe_path
```

## Scatter plots — Chl-a per endosymbiont

```{r cpe-scatter-data}
#| label: cpe-scatter-data

xcpe <- expression("Chl-a per endosymbiont ("*mu*"g cell"^-1*")")

# Tank means (original scale) with chla_per_endo column
tank_means_raw_cpe <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
         cpe = chla_ug_cm2 / endo_per_cm2) |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarize(
    cpe     = mean(cpe,                 na.rm = TRUE),
    endo    = mean(endo_per_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    gp      = mean(GP,                   na.rm = TRUE),
    r       = mean(R,                    na.rm = TRUE),
    biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = max(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(nep) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Fragment-level data (original scale) with cpe column
raw_frag_path_cpe <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
         cpe     = chla_ug_cm2 / endo_per_cm2,
         endo    = endo_per_cm2,
         biomass = mean_tissue_biomass,
         gp      = GP,
         r       = R) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = max(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Beta labels from CPE fit
path_coefs_cpe <- parameterEstimates(cpe_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  dplyr::select(lhs, rhs, std.all, pvalue)

get_beta_label_cpe <- function(response, predictor) {
  row <- path_coefs_cpe |> filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor)
  if (nrow(row) == 0) return("")
  p_str <- if (row$pvalue < 0.001) "p < 0.001" else sprintf("p = %.3f", row$pvalue)
  sprintf("\u03b2 = %.2f, %s", row$std.all, p_str)
}

is_sig_cpe <- function(response, predictor, alpha = 0.05) {
  rows <- path_coefs_cpe |> filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor)
  if (nrow(rows) == 0) return(FALSE)
  rows$pvalue < alpha
}

scatter_cpe <- function(x, y, xlab, ylab,
                        response_var, predictor_var, raw_data,
                        log_x = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  beta_lbl   <- get_beta_label_cpe(response_var, predictor_var)
  sig        <- is_sig_cpe(response_var, predictor_var)
  main_clean <- tank_means_raw_cpe |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))
  raw_clean  <- raw_data           |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))
  x_ann      <- if (log_x) min(main_clean[[x]], na.rm = TRUE) else -Inf

  p <- ggplot(main_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]])) +
    geom_point(data = raw_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]]),
               color = "grey60", alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    { if (sig) geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "grey30", fill = "grey80",
                           linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.25, se = TRUE) } +
    geom_point(aes(fill = TREATMENT), shape = 21, size = 3.2,
               alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0.4, color = "grey20") +
    annotate("text", x = x_ann, y = Inf, label = beta_lbl,
             hjust = -0.08, vjust = 1.5, size = 3.0,
             color = "grey25", fontface = "italic") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title = element_text(size = 9))

  if (log_x) p <- p + scale_x_log10()
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}
```

```{r fig-cpe-scatters}
#| label: fig-cpe-scatters
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 19
#| fig-cap: "Bivariate scatter plots for each direct path in the Chl-a-per-endosymbiont SEM. Grey points show raw fragment-level observations. Coloured circles show tank means (n = 16), filled by treatment. Regression line shown only for significant paths (p < 0.05). Annotated \u03b2 is the standardised path coefficient."

#                     x          y         xlab   ylab   response              predictor           raw_data           log_x  log_y
sc1  <- scatter_cpe("nep",     "ph",      xncp,  xph,  "pH_tank",            "NEP_tank",          raw_chem_path,     FALSE, FALSE)
sc2  <- scatter_cpe("nep",     "doc",     xncp,  xdoc, "DOC_tank",           "NEP_tank",          raw_chem_path,     FALSE, TRUE)
sc3  <- scatter_cpe("ph",      "cpe",     xph,   xcpe, "log_chla_per_endo",  "pH_tank",           raw_frag_path_cpe, FALSE, TRUE)
sc4  <- scatter_cpe("doc",     "cpe",     xdoc,  xcpe, "log_chla_per_endo",  "DOC_tank",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  TRUE)
sc5  <- scatter_cpe("cpe",     "endo",    xcpe,  xendo,"log_endo",           "log_chla_per_endo", raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  TRUE)
sc6  <- scatter_cpe("endo",    "biomass", xendo, xbio, "log_biomass",        "log_endo",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  TRUE)
sc7  <- scatter_cpe("endo",    "r",       xendo, xr,   "mean_R",             "log_endo",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  FALSE)
sc8  <- scatter_cpe("endo",    "gp",      xendo, xgp,  "mean_GP",            "log_endo",          raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  FALSE)
sc9  <- scatter_cpe("biomass", "r",       xbio,  xr,   "mean_R",             "log_biomass",       raw_frag_path_cpe, TRUE,  FALSE)
sc10 <- scatter_cpe("r",       "gp",      xr,    xgp,  "mean_GP",            "mean_R",            raw_frag_path_cpe, FALSE, FALSE)

fig_cpe_scatters <-
  (sc1 | sc2) /
  (sc3 | sc4) /
  (sc5 | sc6) /
  (sc7 | sc8) /
  (sc9 | sc10) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    caption    = paste0(
      "Grey points = raw fragment-level observations  |  ",
      "Coloured circles = tank means (n = 16)  |  ",
      "\u03b2 = standardised path coefficient from Chl-a-per-endosymbiont SEM"
    )
  ) &
  theme(plot.tag     = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 0))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig_cpe_path_scatters.png"),
       fig_cpe_scatters, width = 8, height = 15, dpi = 300)

fig_cpe_scatters
```

## Interpretation — Chl-a per endosymbiont model

### Rationale for the causal structure

This model proposes that the community-level water chemistry signal transmitted to downstream corals operates through per-cell photosynthetic quality rather than total symbiont abundance. The key biological logic has three stages:

**1. pH → Chl-a per cell: photoacclimation under facilitated carbonate chemistry**

Higher seawater pH — driven here by community net community production (NCP) — shifts the carbonate equilibrium towards CO₃²⁻ and HCO₃⁻, increasing the availability of inorganic carbon substrates for photosynthesis [@barott2015]. The coral host actively concentrates CO₂ for symbionts via a vacuolar H⁺-ATPase that acidifies the symbiosome to ~pH 4, functioning as an intracellular carbon concentrating mechanism [@barott2015]. Under higher ambient pH, this pumping mechanism must work harder to create the same local acidification gradient, potentially constraining inorganic carbon supply to individual symbionts. Rather than proliferating in cell number, symbionts may compensate by increasing chlorophyll loading per cell — a well-established photoacclimation response: within 15 days, *Symbiodinium* in corals photoacclimate by modifying chlorophyll a, chlorophyll c₂, and peridinin per cell while maintaining the same pigment ratios [@roth2014]. CO₂ enrichment has been shown to lead to enhanced photoacclimation — specifically increased chlorophyll a per cell — under subsaturating light conditions. The converse is also consistent: at higher pH (lower pCO₂), reduced inorganic carbon availability per cell may require cells to increase antenna size to maximise the conversion efficiency of captured light into fixed carbon.

**2. The chlorophyll packing trade-off: why higher cell density does not co-occur with higher Chl per cell**

The significant negative path from Chl-a/cell → endosymbiont density (β = −0.64) is not a biological causal mechanism but reflects a well-documented optical trade-off. There is a complex relationship between increased pigment loading and the decrease in optical absorption cross-section due to self-shading within the symbiont layer . As cell density increases, individual cells shade one another, making additional chlorophyll per cell increasingly inefficient — an increase in chlorophyll concentration per alga or in algal density are both self-limiting due to the packaging effect. This creates the negative empirical correlation observed here: at high symbiont densities, packing more chlorophyll per cell yields diminishing photosynthetic returns because carbon fixation by individual algal cells decreases with increasing tissue depth due to the attenuation of light from symbiont self-shading . The optimal strategy, therefore, is not to maximise both simultaneously — corals that achieve high cell density produce less chlorophyll per cell, and those with fewer but more heavily pigmented cells achieve similar or greater per-cell photosynthetic output. Chlorophyll concentration per zooxanthella cell and cell density display opposing trends, resulting in relatively homogeneous chlorophyll content per unit area across conditions.

**3. Chl/cell → GP → Tissue biomass & Respiration: the carbon-energy cascade**

Higher chlorophyll per cell increases the light-harvesting capacity of individual symbionts, which should translate to greater gross photosynthesis (GP) if light is not saturating. The downstream consequence is a carbon cascade: endosymbiotic dinoflagellates significantly contribute to the host's nutrition by transferring a large fraction — up to 90% — of their photosynthetically assimilated carbon to support growth, respiration, reproduction, and calcification [@pernice2015; @muscatine1984]. Approximately 38% of daily fixed carbon is translocated to the coral host, fuelling tissue construction and the host's metabolic demands . Tissue biomass therefore accrues as a direct product of the carbon flow from photosynthesis: an increase in translocated photosynthetic products associated with symbiont activity is hypothesised to precede enlargement in tissue biomass .

Respiration scales with this carbon-biomass investment from both directions simultaneously. In low-light environments, corals reduce energetic requirements by decreasing tissue biomass, skeleton thickness, respiration rates, and growth — the inverse pattern of what is expected here . When GP is higher, more photosynthate is available to burn, and a larger tissue mass demands greater maintenance respiration. The simultaneous GP → R and Endo → Biomass paths reflect that under higher photosynthetic output, both the incorporation of carbon into biomass and respiration rates increase together . This is consistent with the idea that respiration is not simply a cost of carbon fixation, but scales with the total living biomass the coral must maintain — the more tissue accumulated (via Endo → Biomass), the higher the ongoing respiratory demand.

### Why this model is structurally distinct from the Chl/cm² model

The Chl/cm² model captures the total pigment signal integrated across all cells, which is driven both by cell number and per-cell loading. The CPE model asks a different question: holding the community context constant, do corals in higher-pH environments achieve their photosynthetic capacity through better-equipped individual symbiont cells, and does this per-cell quality propagate through the carbon economy to tissue growth and respiration? The results offer partial support.

### Big Picture take home  

As coral reefs shift from coral-dominated to algae-dominated states — a trajectory already underway across much of the Indo-Pacific — the benthic community itself becomes a source of modified chemical conditions that feed back on the physiology of any corals that remain. This study shows that community net production is the primary driver of seawater pH across tank treatments, and that this pH signal reaches into coral photobiology by influencing how much chlorophyll each endosymbiont cell carries. The consequence is indirect but structural: because per-cell chlorophyll and symbiont cell density are locked in a strong negative trade-off, any community-driven shift in the chemical environment that pushes symbionts toward greater per-cell pigment investment simultaneously suppresses symbiont proliferation — and since symbiont abundance, not per-cell photosynthetic quality, is the dominant predictor of tissue biomass, the net effect is a reduction in the tissue investment that underpins coral growth, survival, and competitive ability. Critically, the community-to-coral signal in this study was not strong enough to propagate significantly all the way through to gross photosynthesis at n = 16 tanks, suggesting that individual corals retain some metabolic autonomy under moderate community shifts. But this buffering capacity should not be mistaken for resilience to the more severe and sustained phase shifts projected under continued warming and eutrophication: the causal architecture identified here — community metabolism → water chemistry → symbiont photoacclimation → cell density → tissue biomass — represents a plausible mechanism by which degrading reef communities could erode the physiological condition of remnant corals from the outside in, compounding the direct stressors of bleaching and acidification with an indirect chemical legacy of altered benthic composition. This is a chemical legacy effect: degraded communities don't just outcompete corals directly, they rewrite the environment that remaining corals experience.

---

# Fragment-level path analysis with mixed effects {#sec-mixed-path}

## Overview

The main path model (Section [-@sec-path]) operates on **tank-level means** (n = 16). This section re-fits the same causal structure on **individual coral fragments** (n = 74), using mixed-effects models to account for two sources of non-independence in the raw data:

* **Genotype** (`GENOTYPE`): 24 coral genotypes each appear in exactly 4 tanks (one fragment per genotype per tank). Genotype identity explains variation in physiology that is unrelated to the experimental treatment or tank chemistry.
* **Tank** (`TANK_NUM`): 6 fragments share the same tank chemistry (pH, DOC) and the same treatment.

Because the design is fully crossed (each genotype × treatment combination has one fragment), genotype and tank enter as **crossed** random intercepts: `(1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE)`.

The two upstream equations — NCP → pH and NCP → DOC — remain at the tank level (OLS), since both predictor and response are measured per tank. All downstream equations are fragment-level `lmer` models. Path coefficients are standardised (z-scored across all fragments).

::: {.callout-note}
`piecewiseSEM` (Lefcheck 2016), the ideal package for this mixed-level structure, is not available for R 4.3.2. Path equations are instead fitted as separate `lmerTest::lmer()` models; coefficients, Satterthwaite degrees of freedom, and p-values are extracted and assembled into a unified table. Marginal (R²m) and conditional (R²c) R² values follow Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013) and are computed without external packages.
:::

---

## Data preparation

```{r frag-data-prep}
#| label: frag-data-prep

scale_vec <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

# Fragment physiology joined to daytime-pipeline tank chemistry
frag_data_path <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      bind_rows(chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean) |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = mean(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_range = max(pH, na.rm = TRUE) - min(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_min = min(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_max = max(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE)),
        DOC_range = log(max(DOC, na.rm = TRUE) - min(DOC, na.rm= TRUE)),
        DOC_min = min(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_max = max(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(NEP_tank) |>
  mutate(
    log_chla    = log(chla_ug_cm2),
    log_endo    = log(endo_per_cm2),
    cpe = chla_ug_cm2/endo_per_cm2,
    log_biomass = log(mean_tissue_biomass),
    mean_R      = R,
    mean_GP     = GP
  ) |>
  filter(cpe<20)%>%
  drop_na(log_chla, log_endo, log_biomass, mean_GP, mean_R)

# Z-score all continuous variables across the fragment-level dataset
frag_path_scaled <- frag_data_path |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_chla, log_endo, mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

# Unique tank rows for tank-level equations (same scaling)
tank_rows_frag <- frag_path_scaled |>
  distinct(TANK_NUM, NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank)

cat("Fragments in model:", nrow(frag_path_scaled), "\n")
cat("Tanks represented:", n_distinct(frag_path_scaled$TANK_NUM), "\n")
cat("Genotypes represented:", n_distinct(frag_path_scaled$GENOTYPE), "\n")
```

---

## Path equations

```{r frag-path-models}
#| label: frag-path-models

library(lmerTest)

# Tank-level equations (NCP → water chemistry)
#frag_m_ph      <- lm(pH_tank  ~ NEP_tank, data = tank_rows_frag)
#frag_m_doc     <- lm(DOC_tank ~ NEP_tank, data = tank_rows_frag)

# Fragment-level equations: (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE)
frag_m_chla    <- lmer(log_chla    ~ pH_tank  + DOC_tank +
                          (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_endo    <- lmer(log_endo    ~ log_chla + 
                         (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_gp      <- lmer(mean_GP     ~  log_endo + mean_R +
                         (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_r       <- lmer(mean_R      ~ log_biomass  +
                          (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)
frag_m_biomass <- lmer(log_biomass ~ log_endo  +
                          (1|GENOTYPE),
                       data = frag_path_scaled, REML = FALSE)

singular_flags <- sapply(
  list(frag_m_chla, frag_m_endo, frag_m_gp, frag_m_r, frag_m_biomass),
  isSingular
)
cat("Singular fits:", sum(singular_flags), "/ 5 fragment-level models\n")
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Singular fits.** `r sum(singular_flags)` of the 5 fragment-level models are singular, meaning one of the two random-effect variance components is estimated at exactly zero. Inspection of variance components shows that `TANK_NUM` variance collapses to zero in models where the fixed chemistry predictors (pH, DOC) already explain the between-tank variation in the response. The fixed-effect coefficients remain valid; singularity reflects a data limitation rather than a model failure.
:::

---

## Direct path coefficients

```{r frag-path-coefs}
#| label: frag-path-coefs

# Extract standardised coefficients + Satterthwaite df/p
get_frag_coefs <- function(mod, resp, tank = FALSE) {
  s <- as.data.frame(summary(mod)$coefficients) |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Predictor") |>
    dplyr::filter(Predictor != "(Intercept)")
  if (tank) {
    dplyr::transmute(s,
      Response = resp, Predictor,
      `Std. β` = round(Estimate, 3),
      SE       = round(`Std. Error`, 3),
      df       = 14,
      t        = round(`t value`, 3),
      p        = round(`Pr(>|t|)`, 4)
    )
  } else {
    dplyr::transmute(s,
      Response = resp, Predictor,
      `Std. β` = round(Estimate, 3),
      SE       = round(`Std. Error`, 3),
      df       = round(df, 1),
      t        = round(`t value`, 3),
      p        = round(`Pr(>|t|)`, 4)
    )
  }
}

frag_coef_tbl <- bind_rows(
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_chla,    "Chl-a (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_endo,    "Endosymbiont (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_gp,      "Gross photosynthesis"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_r,       "Respiration"),
  get_frag_coefs(frag_m_biomass, "Tissue biomass (log)")
) |>
  mutate(Predictor = recode(Predictor,
    NEP_tank = "NCP",     pH_tank  = "pH",    DOC_tank  = "DOC (log)",
    log_chla = "Chl-a",  log_endo = "Endosymbiont",
    mean_GP  = "GP",     mean_R   = "Respiration",
    log_biomass = "Tissue biomass"
  ))

frag_coef_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Fragment-level path coefficients (n = ", nrow(frag_path_scaled),
      " fragments, n = 16 tanks). All equations use lmer with (1|GENOTYPE) and ",
      "Satterthwaite df. All variables z-scored. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "df", "t", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(frag_coef_tbl$p <  0.05),  background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(frag_coef_tbl$p >= 0.05 & frag_coef_tbl$p < 0.10), background = "#fff3cd")
```

---

## R² for endogenous variables

```{r frag-path-r2}
#| label: frag-path-r2

# Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013): marginal (fixed only) and conditional (fixed + random) R²
r2_NS <- function(mod) {
  vf <- var(as.vector(getME(mod, "X") %*% fixef(mod)))
  vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod))
  vr <- sum(vc$vcov[is.na(vc$var2)])
  ve <- sigma(mod)^2
  c(R2m = round(vf / (vf + vr + ve), 3),
    R2c  = round((vf + vr) / (vf + vr + ve), 3))
}

frag_r2_tbl <- tibble(
  Response   = c("Chl-a (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)",
                 "Gross photosynthesis", "Respiration",
                 "Tissue biomass (log)"),
  `R² / R²m` = c(r2_NS(frag_m_chla)["R2m"],  r2_NS(frag_m_endo)["R2m"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_gp)["R2m"],   r2_NS(frag_m_r)["R2m"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_biomass)["R2m"]),
  `R²c`      = c(r2_NS(frag_m_chla)["R2c"],  r2_NS(frag_m_endo)["R2c"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_gp)["R2c"],   r2_NS(frag_m_r)["R2c"],
                  r2_NS(frag_m_biomass)["R2c"]),
  Singular   = c(isSingular(frag_m_chla), isSingular(frag_m_endo),
                 isSingular(frag_m_gp),   isSingular(frag_m_r),
                 isSingular(frag_m_biomass))
)

frag_r2_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "R\u00b2 per path equation. R\u00b2m (marginal) reflects fixed-effect ",
      "predictors only; R\u00b2c (conditional) includes fixed + random effects ",
      "(Nakagawa & Schielzeth 2013). Singular = one random-effect variance ",
      "component estimated at zero."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "R\u00b2 / R\u00b2m", "R\u00b2c", "Singular fit")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

---

## Random effects variance components

```{r frag-re-variances}
#| label: frag-re-variances

re_tbl <- purrr::map2_dfr(
  list(frag_m_chla, frag_m_endo, frag_m_gp, frag_m_r, frag_m_biomass),
  c("Chl-a (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)", "Gross photosynthesis",
    "Respiration", "Tissue biomass (log)"),
  function(mod, nm) {
    as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod)) |>
      dplyr::filter(is.na(var2)) |>
      dplyr::transmute(
        Response = nm,
        Group    = grp,
        Variance = round(vcov, 4),
        SD       = round(sdcor, 4)
      )
  }
)

re_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Random-effect variance components for each fragment-level equation. Variance \u2248 0 indicates singularity (that component estimated at its boundary).",
    col.names = c("Response", "Group", "Variance", "SD")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

---

## Interpretation

**pH → Chl-a attenuates at the fragment level.** In the tank-level model, pH was a significant predictor of Chl-a (β = 0.49, p = 0.024). In the fragment-level model, this path is weaker and non-significant (β = 0.12, p = 0.28). This attenuation is expected: pH varies only between tanks, whereas genotype explains most of the within-experiment variation in Chl-a (R²c = 0.60 vs R²m = 0.01 — genotype and tank random effects account for the majority of Chl-a variance, leaving little signal for tank-level chemistry at the fragment level). The tank-level model effectively aggregates away this within-tank genotype noise to reveal the between-tank chemistry signal; the fragment-level model shows that at the individual fragment level, the pH signal is diluted by genotypic variation.

**DOC → Endosymbiont remains marginally significant.** The direct negative effect of DOC on endosymbiont density (β = −0.17, p = 0.052) is consistent with the main model, suggesting that this antagonistic route is detectable even after accounting for genotype and tank clustering.

**Downstream physiology paths are robust.** Chl-a → Endosymbiont (β = 0.66, p < 0.001), Endosymbiont → GP (β = 0.35, p = 0.012), GP → Respiration (β = 0.55, p < 0.001), and Endosymbiont → Tissue biomass (β = 0.49, p < 0.001) are all significant with effect sizes consistent with the tank-level analysis. These relationships are driven by within-tank fragment-to-fragment covariation rather than between-tank differences, confirming that the physiology chain is a biological signal rather than an artefact of tank-level averaging.

**Genotype and tank explain substantial variance in some traits.** The large gap between R²m and R²c in Gross photosynthesis (0.16 vs 0.67) and Respiration (0.19 vs 0.60) indicates that substantial between-tank and between-genotype variation exists in these traits, even after controlling for fixed-effect predictors. This is an important caveat: the fragment-level fixed effects are estimated from a relatively small number of effective observations at the tank and genotype levels.

---

# Fragment-level path analysis — Chl-a per endosymbiont {#sec-mixed-cpe}

## Overview

This section repeats the fragment-level mixed-effects path analysis ([Section -@sec-mixed-path]) using **Chl-a per endosymbiont cell** (µg chl cell⁻¹) as the pigment variable, mirroring the tank-level CPE model ([Section -@sec-chl-per-endo]). The causal structure follows the CPE logic: community chemistry (pH, DOC) shapes per-cell pigment content, which then determines symbiont cell density via the self-shading packaging trade-off, and downstream physiology follows from the resulting endosymbiont population. All continuous variables are z-scored; random effects are `(1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE)` for all fragment-level equations.

---

## Data preparation

```{r frag-cpe-data}
#| label: frag-cpe-data

scale_vec <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

frag_cpe_data <- metadata |>
  mutate(
    mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
    cpe = chla_ug_cm2 / endo_per_cm2   # µg chl per cell
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
     # bind_rows(chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean) |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = max(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(max(DOC, na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(NEP_tank) |>
  mutate(
    log_cpe     = log(cpe),
    log_endo    = log(endo_per_cm2),
    log_biomass = log(mean_tissue_biomass),
    mean_R      = R,
    mean_GP     = GP
  ) |>
  drop_na(log_cpe, log_endo, log_biomass, mean_GP, mean_R)

frag_cpe_scaled <- frag_cpe_data |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_cpe, log_endo, mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

tank_rows_cpe <- frag_cpe_scaled |>
  distinct(TANK_NUM, NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank)

cat("Fragments in model:", nrow(frag_cpe_scaled), "\n")
cat("Tanks represented:", n_distinct(frag_cpe_scaled$TANK_NUM), "\n")
cat("Genotypes represented:", n_distinct(frag_cpe_scaled$GENOTYPE), "\n")
```

---

## Path equations

```{r frag-cpe-models}
#| label: frag-cpe-models

library(lmerTest)

cpe_m_cpe     <- lmer(log_cpe     ~ pH_tank + DOC_tank     +  (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_endo    <- lmer(log_endo    ~ log_cpe      + (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_biomass <- lmer(log_biomass ~ log_endo    + (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_r       <- lmer(mean_R      ~ log_biomass  
                      + (1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE)
cpe_m_gp      <- lmer(mean_GP     ~ log_endo + mean_R +(1|GENOTYPE),
                      data = frag_cpe_scaled, REML = FALSE) #mean_R      + 

cpe_frag_mods <- list(cpe=cpe_m_cpe, endo=cpe_m_endo, biomass=cpe_m_biomass,
                      R=cpe_m_r, GP=cpe_m_gp)
cpe_singular  <- sapply(cpe_frag_mods, isSingular)
cat("Singular fits:", sum(cpe_singular), "/ 5 fragment-level models\n")
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Singular fits.** `r sum(cpe_singular)` of the 5 fragment-level CPE models are singular. As in the Chl-a/cm² fragment model, `TANK_NUM` variance collapses to zero when the fixed chemistry predictors already explain the between-tank variation. Coefficients remain interpretable.
:::

---

## Direct path coefficients

```{r frag-cpe-coefs}
#| label: frag-cpe-coefs

get_frag_coefs <- function(mod, resp, tank = FALSE) {
  s <- as.data.frame(summary(mod)$coefficients) |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Predictor") |>
    dplyr::filter(Predictor != "(Intercept)")
  if (tank) {
    dplyr::transmute(s, Response=resp, Predictor,
                     `Std. β`=round(Estimate,3), SE=round(`Std. Error`,3),
                     df=14, t=round(`t value`,3), p=round(`Pr(>|t|)`,4))
  } else {
    dplyr::transmute(s, Response=resp, Predictor,
                     `Std. β`=round(Estimate,3), SE=round(`Std. Error`,3),
                     df=round(df,1), t=round(`t value`,3), p=round(`Pr(>|t|)`,4))
  }
}

cpe_coef_tbl <- bind_rows(
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_cpe,     "Chl-a/cell (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_endo,    "Endosymbiont (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_biomass, "Tissue biomass (log)"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_r,       "Respiration"),
  get_frag_coefs(cpe_m_gp,      "Gross photosynthesis")
) |>
  mutate(Predictor = recode(Predictor,
    NEP_tank="NCP", pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC (log)",
    log_cpe="Chl-a/cell", log_endo="Endosymbiont",
    log_biomass="Tissue biomass", mean_R="Respiration", mean_GP="GP"
  ))

cpe_coef_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Fragment-level CPE path coefficients (n = ", nrow(frag_cpe_scaled),
      " fragments, n = 16 tanks). All equations use lmer with (1|GENOTYPE) and ",
      "Satterthwaite df. All variables z-scored. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "df", "t", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(cpe_coef_tbl$p <= 0.05),  background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(cpe_coef_tbl$p > 0.05 & cpe_coef_tbl$p < 0.10), background = "#fff3cd")
```

---

## Path diagram

```{r frag-cpe-diagram}
#| label: frag-cpe-diagram
```
