```{r frag-cpe-diagram}
#| fig-cap: "Fragment-level CPE path diagram (n = 74 fragments, 16 tanks, 24 genotypes). Green = significant positive path (p < 0.05); red = significant negative path (p < 0.05); grey = n.s. Coefficients are standardised β. R²m (marginal, fixed effects only) shown in each endogenous node."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5

library(purrr)

# Nakagawa-Schielzeth marginal R²
r2_NS <- function(mod) {
  vf <- var(as.vector(getME(mod, "X") %*% fixef(mod)))
  vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod))
  vr <- sum(vc$vcov[is.na(vc$var2)])
  ve <- sigma(mod)^2
  c(R2m = round(vf/(vf+vr+ve), 3), R2c = round((vf+vr)/(vf+vr+ve), 3))
}

# Node positions — pH and DOC are exogenous (no upstream model)
cpe_frag_nodes <- tibble(
  id    = c("pH",  "DOC",  "Chla",            "Endo",                  "Bio",             "R",           "GP"),
  label = c("pH",  "DOC",  "Chl-a\nper cell", "Endosymbiont\ndensity", "Tissue\nbiomass", "Respiration", "Gross\nphotosynthesis"),
  x     = c(1,     1,      3.5,                5.5,                     7.5,               7.5,           9.5),
  y     = c(1.0,  -1.0,    0,                  0,                       1.0,              -0.8,            0.0)
) |>
  mutate(
    r2 = c(NA, NA,
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_cpe)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_endo)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_biomass)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_r)["R2m"]),
           unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_gp)["R2m"])),
    r2_label = ifelse(!is.na(r2), sprintf("R\u00b2=%.2f", r2), NA_character_),
    label_y  = ifelse(!is.na(r2), y + 0.07, y)
  )

# Build edge table from coefficient table
cpe_frag_edges <- tibble(
  from = c("pH","DOC","Chla","Endo","Endo","Bio","Endo","R"),
  to   = c("Chla","Chla","Endo","Bio","R","R","GP","GP")
) |>
  left_join(
    cpe_coef_tbl |>
      mutate(
        from = recode(Predictor,
          NCP="NCP", pH="pH", `DOC (log)`="DOC",
          `Chl-a/cell`="Chla", Endosymbiont="Endo",
          `Tissue biomass`="Bio", Respiration="R"),
        to   = recode(Response,
          pH="pH", `DOC (log)`="DOC", `Chl-a/cell (log)`="Chla",
          `Endosymbiont (log)`="Endo", `Tissue biomass (log)`="Bio",
          Respiration="R", `Gross photosynthesis`="GP")
      ) |>
      dplyr::select(from, to, coef=`Std. β`, p),
    by = c("from","to")
  ) |>
  mutate(colour = case_when(
    p < 0.05 & coef > 0 ~ "pos",
    p < 0.05 & coef < 0 ~ "neg",
    TRUE                  ~ "ns"
  )) |>
  left_join(cpe_frag_nodes |> dplyr::select(id,x,y), by=c("from"="id")) |> rename(x0=x,y0=y) |>
  left_join(cpe_frag_nodes |> dplyr::select(id,x,y), by=c("to"="id"))   |> rename(x1=x,y1=y) |>
  pmap_dfr(function(from,to,coef,p,colour,x0,y0,x1,y1) {
    dx<-x1-x0; dy<-y1-y0; d<-sqrt(dx^2+dy^2)
    tibble(from,to,coef,colour,
           xs=x0+dx/d*0.5, ys=y0+dy/d*0.5,
           xe=x1-dx/d*0.5, ye=y1-dy/d*0.5,
           mx=(x0+dx/d*0.5+x1-dx/d*0.5)/2,
           my=(y0+dy/d*0.5+y1-dy/d*0.5)/2)
  })

colour_vals <- c("pos"="#2ca25f","neg"="#e34a33","ns"="grey70")

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data=cpe_frag_edges,
    aes(x=xs,y=ys,xend=xe,yend=ye,color=colour),
    linewidth=0.85, arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm"),type="closed"),
    show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_label(data=cpe_frag_edges,
    aes(x=mx,y=my,label=sprintf("%.2f",coef),color=colour),
    size=2.5, label.padding=unit(0.10,"lines"), label.size=0,
    fill="white", show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_tile(data=cpe_frag_nodes, aes(x=x,y=y),
    width=0.88, height=0.56,
    fill="white", color="grey35", linewidth=0.55) +
  geom_text(data=cpe_frag_nodes, aes(x=x,y=label_y,label=label),
    size=2.6, lineheight=0.88) +
  geom_text(data=cpe_frag_nodes |> filter(!is.na(r2_label)),
    aes(x=x,y=y-0.13,label=r2_label),
    size=2.0, color="grey40", fontface="italic") +
  scale_color_manual(values=colour_vals) +
  annotate("text", x=0.3, y=-1.6,
    label="Green = sig. positive   Red = sig. negative   Grey = n.s.   Standardised \u03b2   n = 74 fragments",
    hjust=0, size=2.5, color="grey35") +
  coord_equal(xlim=c(0.2,10.2), ylim=c(-1.8,1.8), expand=FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin=margin(6,6,6,6))
```

---

## R² for endogenous variables

```{r frag-cpe-r2}
#| label: frag-cpe-r2

frag_cpe_r2 <- tibble(
  Response   = c("Chl-a/cell (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)",
                 "Tissue biomass (log)", "Respiration",
                 "Gross photosynthesis"),
  `R² / R²m` = c(unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_cpe)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_endo)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_biomass)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_r)["R2m"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_gp)["R2m"])),
  `R²c`      = c(unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_cpe)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_endo)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_biomass)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_r)["R2c"]),
                  unname(r2_NS(cpe_m_gp)["R2c"])),
  Singular   = cpe_singular
)

frag_cpe_r2 |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "R\u00b2 per CPE path equation. R\u00b2m = marginal (fixed effects only); ",
      "R\u00b2c = conditional (fixed + random effects); ",
      "Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013). Singular = one random-effect variance at zero."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "R\u00b2 / R\u00b2m", "R\u00b2c", "Singular fit")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

---

## Random effects variance components

```{r frag-cpe-re}
#| label: frag-cpe-re

purrr::map2_dfr(
  list(cpe_m_cpe, cpe_m_endo, cpe_m_biomass, cpe_m_r, cpe_m_gp),
  c("Chl-a/cell (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)", "Tissue biomass (log)",
    "Respiration", "Gross photosynthesis"),
  function(mod, nm) {
    as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod)) |>
      dplyr::filter(is.na(var2)) |>
      dplyr::transmute(Response=nm, Group=grp,
                       Variance=round(vcov,4), SD=round(sdcor,4))
  }
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Random-effect variance components for the CPE fragment-level equations.",
      col.names = c("Response","Group","Variance","SD")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

---

## Interpretation

**pH → Chl-a/cell is non-significant at the fragment level.** As in the Chl-a/cm² fragment model, the pH signal (β = −0.04, p = 0.76) does not reach per-cell pigment content once genotype variation is accounted for. Marginal R²m for the Chl-a/cell equation is close to zero, whereas R²c is large, indicating that genotype (not tank chemistry) drives most of the variation in per-cell chlorophyll content across fragments.

**The Chl-a/cell → Endosymbiont packaging trade-off is the dominant signal.** The strong negative path (β = −0.64, p < 0.001) replicates the tank-level result and reflects the well-documented self-shading trade-off: corals with higher per-cell chlorophyll harbour fewer endosymbiont cells per unit area. This relationship is primarily driven by fragment-to-fragment covariation and is robust to the inclusion of genotype and tank random effects.

**Downstream physiology is driven by endosymbiont density.** Endosymbiont → Tissue biomass (β = 0.52, p < 0.001), Endosymbiont → Gross photosynthesis (β = 0.34, p < 0.001), and Respiration → Gross photosynthesis (β = 0.47, p < 0.001) are all significant. Biomass → Respiration and Endosymbiont → Respiration are non-significant, suggesting that GP is driven more directly by endosymbiont abundance than by respiratory demand scaling with tissue mass at the fragment level.

**Comparison with the tank-level CPE model.** The fragment-level and tank-level CPE models tell a consistent story for the downstream physiology chain. The main divergence is in the upstream chemistry → Chl-a/cell link, which is significant at the tank level (pH β = 0.49, p = 0.02) but not at the fragment level. This attenuation mirrors the pattern in the Chl-a/cm² fragment model and reflects the fundamental limitation of detecting tank-level chemistry effects in models where genotype dominates within-experiment variation in pigment content.

---

# Supplementary figures {#sec-supp}

## Supp Fig 1 — Diel pH dynamics (24-hr experiment)

```{r load-24hr}
#| label: load-24hr

pHcalib_24 <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)
pHData_24  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "24_hr_carb_chem.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

pH_all_24 <- pHcalib_24 |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = data))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  dplyr::select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData_24, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris    = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(
    pH_lab    = seacarb::pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB),
    pH_insitu = seacarb::pHinsi(pH = pH_lab, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU,
                                Tlab = TEMPINLAB, S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1,
                                k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg")
  )

InflowData_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(InflowTable = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, InflowTable, pH_inflow = pH_insitu)

Data_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID, by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData_24, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE" = "InflowTable")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME  = ymd_hms(paste(as.character(DATE), TIME)),
    pHDiff    = pH_insitu - pH_inflow,
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)
  )
```

```{r fig-supp1}
#| label: fig-supp1
#| fig-cap: "Change in pH relative to inflow across a diel cycle in the 24-hour mesocosm experiment. Each line represents one tank; points show individual measurement times. Yellow shading indicates daytime; grey shading indicates nighttime. The final morning measurement (Jun 03 06:00–09:00) occurred under overcast conditions."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

fig_supp1 <- Data_24 |>
  group_by(DATETIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(avg_pH_diff = mean(pHDiff, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = DATETIME, y = avg_pH_diff)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 12:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#D3D3D3", alpha = 0.4) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 09:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_line(aes(group = TANK_NUM, color = TREATMENT), linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2) +
  annotate("text", x = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 08:00:00"), y = 0.15,
           label = "Overcast\nDay", size = 3.2, color = "grey30") +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M", date_breaks = "6 hours") +
  labs(x = "Date & Time", y = "Change in pH") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig1_diel_pH.png"),
       fig_supp1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_supp1
```

---

## Supp Fig 2 — Coral physiology by treatment

```{r fig-supp2}
#| label: fig-supp2
#| fig-cap: "Coral fragment physiology by community treatment. Large points show treatment means; error bars are ±1 SD; small translucent points are individual fragments. Log₁₀ y-axis for Endosymbiont Density, Chlorophyll-a, and Tissue Biomass. No treatment effect was significant (mixed-effects models with genotype as random intercept, all p > 0.14)."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 9

supp_panel_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

make_physio_panel <- function(yvar, ylab, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(
    metadata |> mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)),
    aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.8,
               position = position_jitter(width = 0.08, seed = 42)) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5) +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, geom = "errorbar",
                 fun.args = list(mult = 1), width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab) +
    supp_panel_theme
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

sp2a <- make_physio_panel("endo_per_cm2",
  expression(bold("Endosymbiont Density") ~ (cells %*% 10^6 ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2b <- make_physio_panel("chla_ug_cm2",
  expression(bold("Chlorophyll-a Content") ~ (µg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2c <- make_physio_panel("mean_tissue_biomass",
  expression(bold("Tissue Biomass") ~ (mg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2d <- make_physio_panel("R",
  expression(bold("Respiration Rate") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))
sp2e <- make_physio_panel("GP",
  expression(bold("Gross Photosynthesis") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))

fig_supp2 <- (sp2a + sp2b + sp2c) / (sp2d + sp2e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig2_physiology_by_treatment.png"),
       fig_supp2, width = 11, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp2
```

---

## Supp Fig 3 — Physiology vs mean pH

```{r fig-supp3}
#| label: fig-supp3
#| fig-cap: "Coral physiology as a function of mean daytime pH (tank means, n = 16). Points colored by treatment. No relationships were significant at α = 0.05 (simple OLS; Chl-a: p = 0.09, Endosymbiont Density: p = 0.84, Tissue Biomass: p = 0.67, Respiration Rate: p = 0.06, Gross Photosynthesis: p = 0.50). Y-axes for Chl-a, Endosymbiont Density, and Tissue Biomass are on a log₁₀ scale."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 9

supp_scatter_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

tank_phys_chem <- raw_frag_path |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(
    mean_chla    = mean(chla_ug_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_endos   = mean(endo_per_cm2,       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R       = mean(R,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP      = mean(GP,                 na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarise(grand_mean_pH  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
                grand_mean_DOC = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

make_scatter <- function(xvar, yvar, xlab, ylab, add_lm = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(tank_phys_chem,
              aes(x = .data[[xvar]], y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    supp_scatter_theme
  if (add_lm) p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
                                    color = "grey30", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8)
  if (log_y)  p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

ph_xlab  <- expression("pH"[T])
chla_ylab  <- expression("Chlorophyll-a Content (" * mu * "g cm"^{-2} * ")")
endo_ylab  <- expression("Endosymbiont Density (cells " * 10^6 ~ "cm"^{-2} * ")")
bio_ylab   <- expression("Mean Tissue Biomass (mg cm"^{-2} * ")")
R_ylab     <- expression("Respiration Rate (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")
GP_ylab    <- expression("Gross Photosynthesis (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")

sp3a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_chla",    ph_xlab, chla_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp3b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_endos",   ph_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp3c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_biomass", ph_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp3d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_R",       ph_xlab, R_ylab)
sp3e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_GP",      ph_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp3 <- (sp3a + sp3b + sp3c) / (sp3d + sp3e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig3_physiology_vs_pH.png"),
       fig_supp3, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp3
```

---

## Supp Fig 4 — Physiology vs mean DOC

```{r fig-supp4}
#| label: fig-supp4
#| fig-cap: "Coral physiology as a function of mean daytime DOC (tank means, n = 16). Points colored by treatment. No significant linear relationships were detected. Y-axes for Chl-a, Endosymbiont Density, and Tissue Biomass are on a log₁₀ scale."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 9

doc_xlab <- expression("DOC (" * mu * "mol L"^{-1} * ")")

sp4a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_chla",    doc_xlab, chla_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_endos",   doc_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_biomass", doc_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp4d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_R",       doc_xlab, R_ylab)
sp4e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_GP",      doc_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp4 <- (sp4a + sp4b + sp4c) / (sp4d + sp4e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig4_physiology_vs_DOC.png"),
       fig_supp4, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp4
```

---

# Model fit comparison {#sec-fit-comparison}

Global fit statistics for all three SEMs, assembled for cross-model comparison.
χ² statistics for small samples (n = 16 tanks) are unreliable in isolation;
the p(χ²), CFI, RMSEA, SRMR, and information criteria together inform adequacy.

```{r all-model-fits}
#| label: all-model-fits

library(kableExtra)

all_models <- list(
  "Main model (Chl/cm²)"        = tank_v6_fit,
  "Sensitivity: excl. 53 & 114" = tank_v6_fit_excl,
  "CPE model (Chl/cell)"        = cpe_fit
)

fit_stats <- c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "tli",
               "rmsea", "rmsea.ci.lower", "rmsea.ci.upper",
               "srmr", "aic", "bic")

fit_comparison <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(all_models), \(nm) {
  fm <- fitMeasures(all_models[[nm]], fit_stats)
  setNames(
    data.frame(matrix(c(nm, round(fm, 3)), nrow = 1)),
    c("Model", fit_stats)
  )
}))

# Rename columns for display
names(fit_comparison) <- c(
  "Model", "\u03c7\u00b2", "df", "p(\u03c7\u00b2)",
  "CFI", "TLI", "RMSEA", "RMSEA\u00a0LB", "RMSEA\u00a0UB",
  "SRMR", "AIC", "BIC"
)

fit_comparison |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Global fit statistics across all three SEMs. ",
      "Acceptable thresholds (at this n): p(\u03c7\u00b2) > 0.05, CFI \u2265 0.90, ",
      "RMSEA \u2264 0.08, SRMR \u2264 0.08. ",
      "All models fall below conventional cut-offs for CFI/RMSEA; ",
      "the non-significant or marginal \u03c7\u00b2 and low \u03c7\u00b2/df ratio remain ",
      "the more informative indicators at n = 16. ",
      "Lower AIC/BIC indicates relatively better fit."
    ),
    align = c("l", rep("r", 11))
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    position   = "left"
  ) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) |>
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Interpreting fit at n = 16:** With only 16 observations the χ²-based
indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA) are known to be highly variable and biased
toward rejection even for well-specified models. The χ²/df ratio
(Main model: 25.1/16 = 1.57; CPE: 31.6/17 = 1.86) and the non-significant
or marginal p(χ²) are the more reliable guides. The main Chl/cm² model
outperforms the CPE model on every metric, including AIC (292.99 vs 294.11),
suggesting per-area pigment is a better-fitting mediator of the community
signal than per-cell pigment under these conditions.
:::

---
